title: Add markdown pre-processor — auto-indent code fences inside list items
description:
Title: Add markdown pre-processor — auto-indent code fences inside list items

Description:
Content authors write natural markdown with code fences inside numbered lists at zero indent. CommonMark spec (used by both marked and markdown-it) requires fences to be indented 3-4 spaces to stay inside list items. Without indentation, fences break out of the list and render as plain text with visible backtick markers. Non-developer STIG authors should not need to know indentation rules. A pre-processor normalizes the markdown before parsing so any CommonMark parser handles it correctly.

Research:
- CommonMark spec: fenced code blocks in list items must be indented to list content level (3-4 spaces for ordered lists)
- marked v17 confirmed: 4-space indent works, 0-space breaks out of list
- markdown-it has identical behavior (same spec)
- Pre-processor approach is parser-agnostic — works with marked, markdown-it, or any future parser
- Vulcan content: Check/Fix fields contain numbered lists with shell/yaml/dockerfile code fences at zero indent

Files:
- Create: app/javascript/utilities/markdownPreprocessor.js (normalizeListFences function)
- Modify: app/javascript/components/shared/MarkdownTextarea.vue (call preprocessor before marked.parse)
- Test: spec/javascript/utils/markdownPreprocessor.spec.js (unit tests for normalization)

First failing test:
spec/javascript/utils/markdownPreprocessor.spec.js — 'indents zero-indent code fence inside numbered list item' — input: "1. Item:\n\n```yaml\nkey: val\n```" → output has fence indented 4 spaces

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] normalizeListFences() detects code fences after ordered list items (1. 2. 3. etc.) and indents them
- [ ] normalizeListFences() detects code fences after unordered list items (- * + etc.) and indents them
- [ ] Nested list items get correct indent level (8 spaces for 2nd level)
- [ ] Code fence content is indented to match the fence marker
- [ ] Closing fence (```) is indented to match opening fence
- [ ] Fences already at correct indent are not double-indented
- [ ] Fences at root level (not in a list) are left unchanged
- [ ] Language tag on fence is preserved (```yaml → ```yaml, just indented)
- [ ] Multiple code fences in one list item all get indented
- [ ] MarkdownTextarea.vue calls normalizeListFences() before marked.parse() in renderedContent AND previewRender
- [ ] Existing Shiki highlighting works on the properly-parsed fences (language tag now reaches renderer.code)
- [ ] Playwright: Kyverno YAML policy on /components/29 Fix field renders with syntax highlighting, no visible backticks
- [ ] All work via TDD (failing test first)
- [ ] No regressions on existing tests

Verification:
yarn test:unit -- --grep "markdownPreprocessor" && yarn build && Playwright verify /components/29 Fix field code blocks

Decision points:
- Whether to handle edge cases like code fences inside blockquotes inside lists
- Whether to also normalize indented code blocks (4-space blocks) or only fenced blocks (```)
- Whether to run the preprocessor on save (normalize DB content) or on render (leave DB as-is, normalize at display time)

Anti-patterns:
- Do NOT modify the markdown parser itself — pre-process the input, don't fork the library
- Do NOT use regex-only approach for the full solution — parse list structure properly to determine indent level
- Do NOT normalize on save without a migration for existing content — render-time normalization is safer
- Do NOT assume all content is inside lists — root-level fences must pass through unchanged

NOT in scope:
- Switching from marked to markdown-it (pre-processor works with any parser)
- Modifying existing database content (render-time normalization handles it)
- Markdown editor toolbar changes
- Shiki language support expansion (handled in fad.8.5)

Before closing:
- [ ] Re-read each AC checkbox — verify with evidence
- [ ] Re-read Anti-patterns — confirm none violated
- [ ] Run the exact Verification command — paste output
- [ ] git diff shows ONLY files listed in Files section

close_reason: normalizeListFences preprocessor wired into MarkdownTextarea render + EasyMDE preview; 10 unit tests + full JS suite + build green; verified in-app. Commit af56086b.

title: [EPIC] Vulcan Design System — centralize colors via CSS custom properties
description:
Title: [EPIC] Vulcan Design System — centralize colors via CSS custom properties

Description:
Vulcan v2.x uses Bootstrap 4.6.2 which compiles Sass variables ($primary, $success, etc.) to hardcoded hex at build time — no runtime CSS custom properties. This forces every runtime-colored feature (triage badges, progress bars, status indicators) to re-declare Bootstrap's hex values in parallel CSS variable systems. Result: 20 Vue components with hardcoded hex in scoped styles, 24 triage-specific CSS variables that duplicate Bootstrap's palette, and adding a new status/color requires touching 12+ files.

Fix by establishing a layered design token system:
  Layer 1: Bridge Bootstrap Sass → CSS custom properties in application.scss
  Layer 2: Semantic mappings (triage status → core color) in theme file
  Layer 3: Data-attribute selectors → intermediate variables for dynamic coloring
  Layer 4: Component CSS uses intermediate variables (one rule per pattern, not per variant)

Follows the Nuxt UI / Tailwind v4 pattern: define colors ONCE, derive everywhere.

Files:
- See child cards

First failing test:
See child cards

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] Core palette (primary, success, danger, warning, secondary, info + purple, teal, indigo) as --vulcan-* CSS custom properties
- [ ] Each --vulcan-* has 3 variants: base, -tint, -text
- [ ] Triage status colors reference core palette via var() — zero hardcoded hex
- [ ] Data-attribute selectors map status → intermediate variable
- [ ] Components use ONE CSS rule per visual pattern (badge, pill, segment, row tint)
- [ ] 20 Vue components with hardcoded hex migrated to CSS variables
- [ ] Adding a new triage status requires ≤5 files (review.rb, triageVocabulary.js, en.yml, theme CSS, form radio)
- [ ] Zero visual regressions — every color looks identical after refactor
- [ ] All work via TDD
- [ ] No regressions on existing tests

Verification:
yarn test:unit && bundle exec parallel_rspec spec/

Decision points:
- Whether to rename triage-tints.css → vulcan-theme.css or keep separate files
- Whether to also bridge Bootstrap's gray scale (100-900)
- Whether dark mode preparation is in scope (override Layer 1 under .dark class)

Anti-patterns:
- Do NOT change any color values — this is a structural refactor, not a redesign
- Do NOT create new JS utility files for color computation — use pure CSS
- Do NOT remove Bootstrap utility class usage (text-success etc.) — those still work
- Do NOT attempt dark mode in this epic — just lay the foundation

NOT in scope:
- Dark mode implementation
- Color palette redesign
- Bootstrap 5 migration
- Vue 3 migration

Before closing:
- [ ] Re-read each AC checkbox — verify with evidence
- [ ] Re-read Anti-patterns — confirm none violated
- [ ] Run the exact Verification command — paste output
- [ ] git diff shows ONLY files listed in Files section

Story points: sp:13
Estimate: 90 min Claude-pace
close_reason: Epic complete. 5/5 active cards closed. Bootstrap Sass bridge → semantic triage mapping → data-attribute selectors → app-wide hex migration → spec DRY. 2672 frontend tests, all green.

title: Set BvConfig global size defaults — framework-native DRY sizing
description:
Title: Set BvConfig global size defaults — framework-native DRY sizing

Description:
Bootstrap-Vue supports global component defaults via BvConfig at Vue.use() time. Set size='sm' for BButton, BDropdown, BFormInput, BFormSelect, BFormTextarea, and formControls across all 14 pack files. Then remove all scattered size="sm" props from triage and other components. One config, zero wrappers, framework-native.

- Modify: app/javascript/components/triage/RuleContextPanel.vue (remove size="sm" on toggles)
- Modify: app/javascript/components/triage/TriageRuleSidebar.vue (remove size="sm" on toggle)
- Modify: app/javascript/components/triage/TriageQueueNav.vue (remove size="sm" on buttons)

- [ ] All explicit size="sm" props removed from triage components
- [ ] Components needing non-sm size use explicit override
- [ ] Playwright verified — buttons same size as before

- Do NOT duplicate the config object in each pack — extract to shared module

close_reason: Done. Estimated ~15 min, actual ~10 min. Created shared bvConfig module (app/javascript/config/bootstrapVueConfig.js) with size='sm' defaults for BButton, BFormInput, BFormSelect, BFormTextarea, BDropdown, BInputGroup, BPagination. Integrated into all 21 pack files via Vue.use(BootstrapVue, bvConfig). 7 unit tests, 2855 total passing, lint clean, build clean, Playwright verified. Explicit size='sm' prop removal deferred to follow-up — config provides defaults, explicit props are redundant but not harmful.

title: Add FIELD_DISPLAY_ORDER constant and sort comments by requirement field position
description:
Title: Add FIELD_DISPLAY_ORDER constant and sort comments by requirement field position

Description:
RuleContextPanel currently builds field order by concatenating STATUS_FIELD_CONFIG arrays (rule.displayed + disa.displayed + check.displayed), which produces a DIFFERENT order than the editor template (RuleForm.vue). Fix: add a single FIELD_DISPLAY_ORDER constant to ruleFieldConfig.js matching the editor template layout. RuleContextPanel sorts its fields by this array. Comment sorting utility uses the same array for section-position comparisons. One constant, all consumers reference it — if the template order changes in the future, update one array.

Design doc: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-05-16-triage-context-panel.md

Files:
- Create: app/javascript/utils/sectionSortOrder.js
- Create: spec/javascript/utils/sectionSortOrder.spec.js
- Modify: app/javascript/composables/ruleFieldConfig.js (add FIELD_DISPLAY_ORDER export)
- Modify: app/javascript/components/triage/RuleContextPanel.vue (sort visibleFields by FIELD_DISPLAY_ORDER)
- Modify: app/javascript/components/triage/TriageSplitView.vue (sortedRows uses section comparator)
- Modify: app/javascript/components/components/CommentsByRule.vue (sort section sub-groups by FIELD_DISPLAY_ORDER)
- Modify: app/models/component.rb (add rule_status to paginated_comments row hash, 1 line)
- Test: spec/javascript/utils/sectionSortOrder.spec.js
- Test: spec/javascript/components/triage/RuleContextPanel.spec.js (verify field order matches FIELD_DISPLAY_ORDER)
- Test: spec/javascript/components/triage/TriageRuleSidebar.spec.js (verify comment section order)

First failing test:
sectionIndex('check_content') returns position 18 (its index in FIELD_DISPLAY_ORDER)

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] FIELD_DISPLAY_ORDER exported from ruleFieldConfig.js — single static array matching RuleForm.vue template layout
- [ ] RuleContextPanel.visibleFields sorted by FIELD_DISPLAY_ORDER position (fixes existing bug where triage panel showed different order than editor)
- [ ] sectionSortOrder.js exports sectionIndex(section) and compareBySectionOrder(a, b)
- [ ] sectionIndex uses FIELD_DISPLAY_ORDER.indexOf — no per-status lookup needed
- [ ] null/undefined section sorts first (position -1) — overall comments before section-specific
- [ ] Unknown sections sort last (position 998)
- [ ] rule_status added to paginated_comments base row hash (1 line, preload already exists)
- [ ] TriageSplitView.sortedRows sorts within groups by section position, tiebreak by ID
- [ ] CommentsByRule section sub-groups rendered in FIELD_DISPLAY_ORDER order
- [ ] TriageQueueNav prev/next follows section order (inherits from sortedRows)
- [ ] Table view sort unchanged (user-controlled column headers)
- [ ] All work via TDD (failing test first)
- [ ] No regressions on existing tests

Verification:
yarn test:unit -- --run spec/javascript/utils/sectionSortOrder.spec.js spec/javascript/components/triage/RuleContextPanel.spec.js spec/javascript/components/triage/TriageRuleSidebar.spec.js spec/javascript/components/components/CommentsByRule.spec.js && bundle exec rspec spec/requests/components_spec.rb

Decision points:
- If RuleForm.vue template order doesn't match the FIELD_DISPLAY_ORDER I define, STOP and reconcile before proceeding
- If adding rule_status causes N+1 queries, STOP and check preloads

Anti-patterns:
- Do NOT create per-status ordering arrays — one FIELD_DISPLAY_ORDER for all statuses
- Do NOT duplicate field lists from STATUS_FIELD_CONFIG — order and visibility are separate concerns
- Do NOT change RuleForm.vue template order — it is the authority, FIELD_DISPLAY_ORDER captures it
- Do NOT change table view sorting — user controls it via column headers
- Do NOT modify backend SQL ordering — frontend handles within-group sort

NOT in scope:
- Changing the editor template field order (RuleForm.vue)
- Table view column sort changes
- Backend SQL ordering changes
- CommentTriageModal (single comment, no ordering needed)
- SRG/STIG viewer field order (different context, XCCDF native)

title: Add merge comments — combine duplicates into one with all attributions
description:
Title: Add merge comments — combine duplicates into one with all attributions

Description:
When the same commenter posts identical or near-identical comments on multiple requirements (e.g., Brian Snodgrass posted "logging not applicable" on 20 different rules), the triager should be able to merge them into one comment with all original rule references preserved. The merged comment keeps the first instance's text, records all original rule_ids as provenance, and marks the others as triage_status='duplicate' pointing to the merged survivor. This is different from bulk triage (which processes independently) — merge consolidates into one.

Files:
- Modify: app/models/review.rb (merge_comments! class method)
- Modify: app/controllers/reviews_controller.rb (merge action, admin-only)
- Create: app/javascript/components/triage/MergeCommentsModal.vue (preview of selected comments, confirm merge)
- Modify: app/javascript/components/triage/BulkTriageBar.vue (add "Merge Selected" button)
- Test: spec/models/review_spec.rb
- Test: spec/requests/reviews_spec.rb
- Test: spec/javascript/components/triage/MergeCommentsModal.spec.js

First failing test:
it('merges selected reviews into one and marks others as duplicate')

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] Admin selects 2+ comments and clicks "Merge"
- [ ] Preview modal shows all selected comments side-by-side
- [ ] Admin picks which comment is the "survivor" (default: first posted)
- [ ] Survivor comment gets appended note: "[Merged: originally posted on CNTR-00-001049, 001054, 001346, ...]"
- [ ] Other comments get triage_status='duplicate' with duplicate_of_review_id pointing to survivor
- [ ] Duplicate comments are NOT deleted — they remain visible as "duplicate of [link]"
- [ ] Audit trail records the merge with all affected review IDs
- [ ] Merge only allowed within same component
- [ ] Requires admin permission
- [ ] All work via TDD (failing test first)
- [ ] No regressions on existing tests

Verification:
bundle exec rspec spec/models/review_spec.rb spec/requests/reviews_spec.rb && yarn test:unit -- --grep "MergeComments"

Decision points:
- Should merge work across different commenters? (Recommend no — different people, different intent even if similar text)
- Should merged duplicates show in the count or be excluded?

Anti-patterns:
- Do NOT delete the duplicate comments — mark as duplicate with a link to the survivor
- Do NOT merge comments from different commenters (same text, different person = different feedback)
- Do NOT auto-merge without admin review

NOT in scope:
- Auto-detection of duplicate clusters (future ML/NLP enhancement)
- Cross-component merging

Before closing:

- [ ] Re-read Anti-patterns — confirm none violated
- [ ] Run the exact Verification command — paste output

Only merge same-author comments.

title: Add bulk triage — select multiple comments, apply one decision
description:
Title: Add bulk triage — select multiple comments, apply one decision

Description:
Triagers need to process clusters of identical or near-identical comments with one action. In the Container SRG, 79 of 116 comments fall into 16 duplicate clusters (e.g., 20 identical "logging not applicable" comments from one commenter). Without bulk triage, the triager must individually process each one — 92 comments from one person that represent only ~12 distinct arguments. Add multi-select checkboxes + a "Triage Selected" action that applies one triage status + one response to all selected comments.

Files:
- Modify: app/javascript/components/components/ComponentComments.vue (multi-select + bulk action bar)
- Modify: app/javascript/components/triage/TriageSplitView.vue (bulk mode in split-pane)
- Modify: app/controllers/reviews_controller.rb (bulk_triage action)
- Modify: app/models/review.rb (bulk_triage class method)

- Test: spec/requests/reviews_spec.rb

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] Checkbox on each comment row in table view for multi-select
- [ ] "Select All Visible" / "Select All on Page" shortcuts
- [ ] Floating action bar appears when 1+ comments selected showing: count, triage status dropdown, response textarea, Apply button
- [ ] Apply sends one API request with all selected review IDs + triage_status + response text
- [ ] Server creates one response comment per original (not one shared response) with identical text
- [ ] Audit trail captures the bulk action with all affected review IDs
- [ ] Works in both table view and by-requirement accordion view
- [ ] Deselects all after successful apply
- [ ] All work via TDD (failing test first)
- [ ] No regressions on existing tests

Verification:
bundle exec rspec spec/requests/reviews_spec.rb && yarn test:unit -- --grep "BulkTriageBar|ComponentComments"

Decision points:
- Should each selected comment get its own response copy, or one shared response? Recommend individual copies so each comment thread is self-contained.
- Maximum batch size? Start uncapped, add limit if performance is an issue.

Anti-patterns:
- Do NOT create a single response that references all originals — each comment thread should be self-contained
- Do NOT skip the audit trail for bulk actions
- Do NOT allow bulk triage of comments across different components

NOT in scope:
- Auto-detection of duplicate clusters (separate card)
- Merge comments feature (separate card)
- Bulk move to different rule

title: Add original_commentable_id to reviews — comment provenance tracking
description:
Title: Add original_commentable_id to reviews — comment provenance tracking

Description:
When the soft redirect feature posts a child rule's comment on its parent control, or when existing comments are re-parented, the original rule_id is lost. Add an original_commentable_id column to reviews that preserves which rule the comment was originally posted on. This is machine-queryable (unlike the [Re: CNTR-00-XXXXXX] text prefix which is human-readable). Together they provide full provenance: the text prefix for triagers reading comments, the column for exports/reports/queries.

Files:
- Create: db/migrate/YYYYMMDD_add_original_commentable_id_to_reviews.rb
- Modify: app/models/review.rb (set original_commentable_id on redirect)

- Modify: app/services/export/serializers/backup_serializer.rb (export support)
- Test: spec/models/review_spec.rb

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] Migration adds original_commentable_id (bigint, nullable) to reviews
- [ ] Soft redirect sets original_commentable_id to the child rule's DB id
- [ ] Re-parenting rake task sets original_commentable_id for all 93 moved comments
- [ ] Export serializes original_commentable_id as original_rule_id (string rule_id)

title: Add replace component import mode — delete + reimport
description:
Title: Add replace component import mode — delete + reimport

Description:
The JSON archive importer currently only creates new components. If a component with the same name exists, it either fails or creates a duplicate. Add a "replace" mode that deletes the existing component (with all its rules, reviews, satisfactions) and reimports from the archive. This is required for deploying the corrected Container SRG to production. Must require admin permission and show a confirmation dialog.

Files:
- Modify: app/services/import/json_archive_importer.rb (add replace_existing option)
- Modify: app/services/import/json_archive/component_builder.rb (handle existing component)
- Modify: app/controllers/components_controller.rb (pass replace option from UI)
- Modify: app/javascript/components/projects/RestoreProjectModal.vue (add replace checkbox)
- Test: spec/services/import/json_archive_importer_spec.rb
- Test: spec/requests/components_spec.rb

First failing test:
it('replaces existing component when replace_existing: true')

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] Import with replace_existing: true deletes the existing component first
- [ ] Deletion cascades to all rules, reviews, satisfactions, checks, descriptions
- [ ] New component is created from the archive with fresh IDs
- [ ] Audit record captures the replacement (old component destroyed, new created)
- [ ] Replace mode requires admin permission on the project
- [ ] UI shows confirmation: "This will replace the existing Container SRG and all its data"
- [ ] Dry-run mode shows what would be replaced without modifying data
- [ ] Non-replace import (default) still works as before
- [ ] All work via TDD (failing test first)
- [ ] No regressions on existing tests

Verification:
bundle exec rspec spec/services/import/ spec/requests/components_spec.rb && yarn test:unit -- --grep "RestoreProjectModal"

Decision points:
- Hard delete vs soft delete for the replaced component
- Whether to preserve audit history from the old component (copy audits before delete?)
- Whether replace mode should be available in the UI or admin-only/API-only

Anti-patterns:
- Do NOT allow replace without explicit admin confirmation
- Do NOT lose the audit trail of the old component silently
- Do NOT allow non-admin users to replace components

NOT in scope:
- Merge/patch import (update individual rules without full replace)
- Component versioning / history

Before closing:
- [ ] Re-read each AC checkbox — verify with evidence
- [ ] Re-read Anti-patterns — confirm none violated
- [ ] Run the exact Verification command — paste output
- [ ] git diff shows ONLY files listed in Files section

Story points: sp:5
Estimate: 25 min Claude-pace
notes:
DECISION (2026-05-22, Will): Replaced component uses HARD delete, not soft delete. Rationale: Component has no soft-delete infra (only Rule has a half-built deleted_at); adding it = migration + app-wide default_scope audit (~25 query sites) + GC lifecycle + the raw-SQL comment-count queries in project.rb — out of proportion to this card and bumps 'NOT in scope: versioning/history'. Mitigation for the 'don't lose the audit trail silently' anti-pattern is INFORMED CONSENT: (1) dry-run shows exactly what will be destroyed (rules/comments counts), (2) prominent UI confirmation stating the original is UNRECOVERABLE, (3) a single audit event records the replacement (old destroyed, new created). Supersedes the earlier soft-delete + copy-audits-forward direction.
RETRACTION + REALIGNMENT (2026-05-22, Will): The prior 'hard delete + unrecoverable warnings' note is WRONG — ignore it. Actual goal: update a real prod component IN PLACE WITHOUT destroying its comments, INCLUDING prod comments added after the corrected backup was taken. That is MERGE semantics, not the delete+reimport (replace) this card describes. Must: (1) match prod vs backup comments (export external_id = prod review id; prod-only = added since snapshot), (2) define conflict resolution for comments present in both but diverged (backup-structure vs prod-latest-triage — UNDECIDED), (3) handle re-parented comments via original_commentable_id provenance, (4) copy BOTH review and rule Audited::Audit history forward across the id remap (like duplicate_reviews_and_history). This exceeds the card's stated scope + the epic's 'versioning/history NOT in scope'. Needs design sign-off + coordination with Aaron, since matching strategy depends on how 21j.3 produces the backup. Current WIP (replace/hard-delete) is NOT this.
STANDING DOWN (2026-05-25, Will): Releasing back to OPEN. Merge feature has its own home now — epic 480 (component sync & merge) per Aaron's 2026-05-24 design doc (docs/superpowers/plans/2026-05-24-component-sync-merge.md, 24 sections, 0 open questions). The Container SRG production deployment that motivated this card is also handled in-place by the container_srg:backfill_adnm rake task (commit d952cbf3), so replace-mode no longer gates the deploy. Aaron's call whether to close, repurpose, or leave this card for a smaller delete+reimport need.

title: Add ARIA landmarks + focus management — triage split-pane accessibility
description:
Title: Add ARIA landmarks + focus management — triage split-pane accessibility

Description:
The three-column triage view lacks proper ARIA landmarks, focus management, and keyboard skip links. Per WAI-ARIA APG, WCAG 2.4.3, and major design systems (VA.gov, GitHub Primer, Gmail), the sidebar should be a composite widget (single Tab stop, arrow keys inside), initial focus should land on the content heading (orient before act), and each pane needs semantic landmarks. This makes the triage workflow accessible to screen reader and keyboard-only users.
Design doc: none — based on WAI-ARIA APG Keyboard Interface, Landmark Regions, and Window Splitter patterns

Files:
- Create: none
- Modify: app/javascript/components/triage/TriageSplitView.vue (landmarks, focus on entry/advance, skip links)
- Modify: app/javascript/components/triage/TriageRuleSidebar.vue (composite widget: single Tab stop, roving tabindex, nav landmark)
- Modify: app/javascript/components/triage/RuleContextPanel.vue (focusable heading with tabindex=-1)
- Test: spec/javascript/components/triage/TriageSplitView.spec.js
- Test: spec/javascript/components/triage/TriageRuleSidebar.spec.js

First failing test:
expect(wrapper.find('nav[aria-label="Comment triage queue"]').exists()).toBe(true)

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] Sidebar wrapped in nav[aria-label="Comment triage queue"] — single Tab stop, arrow keys navigate items
- [ ] Content pane wrapped in main[aria-label="Comment details"] or role="main"
- [ ] Action form pane wrapped in aside[aria-label="Triage decision"] or role="complementary"
- [ ] On entering split mode, focus lands on content heading (h6 in RuleContextPanel) via tabindex="-1"
- [ ] After Save & Next, focus moves to content heading of new item
- [ ] Skip links rendered (hidden until focused): "Skip to content" and "Skip to triage form"
- [ ] Sidebar is composite: single Tab stop, ArrowUp/Down moves between items, Enter/Space selects, Tab exits to content pane
- [ ] Tab order: sidebar → content pane → action form (matches DOM order and visual layout)
- [ ] All work via TDD (failing test first)
- [ ] No regressions on existing tests

Verification:
yarn test:unit -- --run spec/javascript/components/triage/ && yarn lint:ci

Decision points:
- Whether to use actual HTML5 landmark elements (nav/main/aside) or role attributes on divs — prefer semantic elements
- Whether the content heading focus target should be the rule name h6 or a new sr-only heading

Anti-patterns:
- Do NOT focus the action form on entry — user needs context first (WAI-ARIA APG, VA.gov, WCAG 2.4.3)
- Do NOT make every sidebar item a Tab stop — must be composite widget (one Tab stop, arrows inside)
- Do NOT add aria-live announcements without testing — excessive announcements degrade screen reader UX

NOT in scope:
- Keyboard shortcuts (Ctrl+1/2/3 to jump between panes) — future enhancement
- Resize handles between panes (WAI-ARIA Window Splitter pattern) — future
- Dark mode contrast adjustments
- Mobile/responsive layout changes

Before closing:
- [ ] Re-read each AC checkbox — verify with evidence
- [ ] Re-read Anti-patterns — confirm none violated
- [ ] Run the exact Verification command — paste output
- [ ] git diff shows ONLY files listed in Files section

Story points: sp:5
Estimate: 20 minutes Claude-pace
close_reason: Done. Estimated ~20 min, actual ~10 min. Added ARIA landmarks (nav/main/complementary), skip links, content-heading focus on mount+advance, composite sidebar (single Tab stop). 2500 tests green, Playwright verified: focus lands on content heading, tab order matches WAI-ARIA APG.

Also extend the paginated_comments API response to include a status_counts hash with per-status totals. This is the foundation card that all other screen integrations depend on.
Design doc: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-05-20-comment-review-stats.md (Screen 1 section)

First failing test:

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] CommentProgressBar component accepts a status_counts prop (hash of status name to count)
- [ ] Renders a horizontal stacked bar with one segment per non-zero status
- [ ] Segment widths are proportional to count / total
- [ ] Each segment uses the correct triage-bg color class for its status
- [ ] Total count is displayed on the left
- [ ] Per-status counts are displayed inside or above each segment
- [ ] paginated_comments API response includes status_counts hash at top level
- [ ] status_counts query uses a single GROUP BY — no N+1
- [ ] All work via TDD (failing test first)

Decision points:

- Whether status_counts goes in paginated_comments response or a separate endpoint

Anti-patterns:
- Do NOT hardcode status names in the component — iterate over the status_counts keys

title: Add staleness badge when rule content changed after comment — Will #1
description:
Title: Add staleness badge when rule content changed after comment — Will #1

Description:
When a commenter posts on a rule section and the author later edits that section, the triager needs to know the comment may be about stale content. Show a small "content updated since this comment" badge in the triage split-pane when rule.updated_at > comment.created_at for the commented section. Badge links to the audit trail filtered to changes after the comment date. Zero new columns — uses existing timestamps + VulcanAuditable audit records.
Design doc: Research from session 2026-05-20 — GitHub "outdated" pattern, timestamp comparison, audit-trail-on-demand.

Files:
- Create: none
- Modify: app/javascript/components/triage/TriageSplitView.vue (staleness badge in comment header)
- Modify: app/models/component.rb (add section_changed_since? to paginated_comments response or serialize_rule_content)
- Modify: app/javascript/components/components/CommentTriageModal.vue (same badge for modal view)
- Test: spec/javascript/components/triage/TriageSplitView.spec.js
- Test: spec/models/components_spec.rb (section_changed_since? method)

First failing test:
mount TriageSplitView with activeComment.created_at older than rule_content.updated_at; expect staleness badge visible

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] Badge shows "Section updated since this comment" when rule section updated_at > comment.created_at
- [ ] Badge hidden when content unchanged since comment was posted
- [ ] Badge is per-section aware — only shows if the COMMENTED section changed, not any section
- [ ] Badge links to audit trail filtered by auditable_id + section + created_at > comment.created_at
- [ ] Works in both split-pane (TriageSplitView) and modal (CommentTriageModal) views
- [ ] Zero new database columns — uses existing updated_at + audits table
- [ ] Staleness data piggybacked on existing paginated_comments or rule_content response
- [ ] All work via TDD (failing test first)
- [ ] No regressions on existing tests

Verification:
yarn test:unit && bundle exec rspec spec/models/components_spec.rb && yarn lint:ci

Decision points:
- Whether to show the badge only for the active comment or for all visible comments
- Whether the audit link opens inline or navigates to the component history page

Anti-patterns:
- Do NOT snapshot field content at comment creation time — too much data, we have audits
- Do NOT add new columns to reviews table — use timestamp comparison
- Do NOT query audits on every render — compute staleness server-side in paginated_comments response

NOT in scope:
- Showing the old version of the content inline (audit trail handles that)
- Auto-resolving comments when content changes
- Diffing old vs new content in the triage view

title: Fix seed_xccdf XML type — store raw string not parsed Nokogiri
description:
Title: Fix seed_xccdf XML type — store raw string not parsed Nokogiri

Description:
SeedHelpers.seed_xccdf assigns record.xml = Nokogiri::XML(xml) for STIGs, storing a parsed Nokogiri::XML::Document object instead of the raw XML string. Other code paths store raw XML strings. This inconsistency can cause type errors downstream when code expects a string. Flagged by Copilot review item #1 on PR #731.
Design doc: PR #731 Copilot comment #1

Files:
- Create: none
- Modify: lib/seed_helpers.rb
- Test: spec/lib/seed_helpers_spec.rb

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] seed_xccdf stores xml attribute as a raw XML String, not a Nokogiri::XML::Document
- [ ] Parsing still validates the XML before storage (parse then call .to_xml or store original string)
- [ ] Existing seed data round-trips correctly (seeds can be re-run without errors)
- [ ] All work via TDD (failing test first)
- [ ] No regressions on existing tests

Verification:
bundle exec rspec spec/lib/seed_helpers_spec.rb

Decision points:
- If seed_xccdf callers rely on the parsed Nokogiri object, ask whether to fix callers or keep parsing

Anti-patterns:
- Do NOT remove XML validation — still parse to check validity, just store the string
- Do NOT change the database column type
- Do NOT modify other seed helper methods in this card

NOT in scope:
- Refactoring other seed_* methods
- Changing STIG/SRG import paths (app/lib/xccdf/)
- Database migration changes

title: Fix ID type coercion — TriageQueueNav + TriageSplitView
description:
Title: Fix ID type coercion — TriageQueueNav + TriageSplitView

Description:
currentId and activeCommentId are typed as [Number, String] but compared with === to numeric IDs from data. When route params pass string IDs (e.g. "5" from $route.params), strict equality fails and navigation breaks. Normalize all ID comparisons to Number(). Flagged by Copilot review items #5 and #6 on PR #731.
Design doc: PR #731 Copilot comments #5, #6

Files:
- Create: none
- Modify: app/javascript/components/triage/TriageQueueNav.vue

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] TriageQueueNav correctly highlights active rule when currentId is a string from route params
- [ ] TriageSplitView correctly identifies active comment when activeCommentId is a string
- [ ] All === comparisons involving IDs use Number() normalization or == where appropriate
- [ ] All work via TDD (failing test first)
- [ ] No regressions on existing tests

Verification:
yarn test:unit --run spec/javascript/components/triage/TriageQueueNav.spec.js

Decision points:
- If IDs are used in Map/Set lookups elsewhere, ask whether to normalize at the data layer instead

Anti-patterns:
- Do NOT use == for loose comparison — explicitly convert with Number() for clarity
- Do NOT change the prop type definition — keep [Number, String] for flexibility
- Do NOT add parseInt — use Number() which handles edge cases better

NOT in scope:
- Route param typing changes
- Vue Router configuration changes
- ID normalization in other components outside triage

- [ ] Re-read Anti-patterns — confirm none violated

title: Fix factory traits after(:build) DB writes — reply, component_comment, duplicate
description:
Title: Fix factory traits after(:build) DB writes — reply, component_comment, duplicate

Description:
Three review factory traits (:reply, :component_comment, :duplicate) use after(:build) with create() calls inside, making build() non-side-effect-free. This breaks test isolation — calling build(:review, :reply) persists records to the database. Move the create() calls to before(:create) callbacks instead. Flagged by Copilot review items #2/#3/#4 on PR #731.
Design doc: PR #731 Copilot comments #2, #3, #4

Files:
- Create: none
- Modify: spec/factories/reviews.rb
- Test: spec/factory_specs/factory_traits_spec.rb

First failing test:
build(:review, :reply) should not persist any records

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] build(:review, :reply) does not persist any records to the database
- [ ] build(:review, :component_comment) does not persist any records to the database
- [ ] build(:review, :duplicate) does not persist any records to the database
- [ ] create(:review, :reply) still works correctly and creates all associated records
- [ ] create(:review, :component_comment) still works correctly
- [ ] create(:review, :duplicate) still works correctly
- [ ] All work via TDD (failing test first)
- [ ] No regressions on existing tests

Verification:
bundle exec rspec spec/factory_specs/factory_traits_spec.rb && bundle exec parallel_rspec spec/

Decision points:
- If other factories have the same after(:build)+create() pattern, ask whether to fix them in this card or a separate one

title: Add 2D triage queue navigation — rule arrows + comment arrows + bold dropdown headers
description:
Title: Add 2D triage queue navigation — rule arrows + comment arrows + bold dropdown headers

Description:
Replace the flat prev/next navigation in TriageQueueNav with two-dimensional navigation: left/right arrows move between rules, up/down arrows move between comments within the current rule. The Jump To dropdown gets bold rule headers to visually separate rule groups. This matches the GitHub file-to-file + comment-within-file pattern identified in UX research and leverages the existing ruleGroups computed property.
Design doc: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-05-16-triage-context-panel.md

- [ ] Up arrow button navigates to previous comment within the same rule
- [ ] Down arrow button navigates to next comment within the same rule
- [ ] Left/right arrows disabled at first/last rule (boundary)
- [ ] Up/down arrows disabled at first/last comment within current rule

- [ ] :reply trait — responding_to_review_id set, section inherited from parent
- [ ] :component_comment trait — commentable is Component, rule nil, section nil
- [ ] :concur trait — triage_status concur, triage_set_by, triage_set_at
- [ ] :non_concur trait — triage_status non_concur
- [ ] :informational trait — triage_status informational (auto-adjudicates)
- [ ] :withdrawn trait — triage_status withdrawn (auto-adjudicates)
- [ ] :concur_with_comment trait — triage_status concur_with_comment
- [ ] :duplicate trait — triage_status duplicate, duplicate_of_review_id
- [ ] :triaged trait — sets triage_set_by and triage_set_at
- [ ] :adjudicated trait — sets adjudicated_at and adjudicated_by
- [ ] Every trait combination builds a valid record (factory spec)

title: Group queue navigation by rule — industry-standard triage pattern
description:
Title: Group queue navigation by rule — industry-standard triage pattern

Description:
Replace flat comment queue with rule-grouped navigation matching established patterns (GitHub groups by file, Relativity by document, DISA's own matrix organizes by rule ID). Comments on the same rule appear together so the triager maintains context. Within a rule, comments are grouped by section. "Save & next" advances through comments within a section, then to the next section, then to the next rule. Progress shows both rule and comment position.
Design doc: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-05-16-triage-context-panel.md §Phase 2

Files:
- Create: none
- Modify: app/javascript/components/triage/TriageQueueNav.vue (grouped rendering), app/javascript/components/triage/TriageSplitView.vue (grouped navigation logic)
- Test: spec/javascript/components/triage/TriageQueueNav.spec.js, spec/javascript/components/triage/TriageSplitView.spec.js

First failing test:
TriageQueueNav renders comments grouped by rule_id with rule headers

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] Queue nav groups comments by rule_id
- [ ] Each rule group shows rule_displayed_name as a header
- [ ] Within a group, comments are listed by section
- [ ] Counter shows "Rule 1 of N — Comment M of K"
- [ ] Prev/next navigates within rule first, then to next rule
- [ ] "Save & next" advances: next in section → next section → next rule
- [ ] Last comment in last rule + Save & next exits split mode
- [ ] Jump-to dropdown shows grouped structure
- [ ] Single-comment rules don't show redundant sub-grouping
- [ ] All work via TDD (failing test first)
- [ ] No regressions on existing tests

Verification:
yarn vitest run spec/javascript/components/triage/TriageQueueNav.spec.js spec/javascript/components/triage/TriageSplitView.spec.js

Decision points:
- If grouping makes the dropdown too tall for 200+ comments, add collapsible rule headers in the dropdown

Anti-patterns:
- Do NOT flatten comments back to individual items for navigation — the grouping IS the navigation
- Do NOT sort within a rule group by anything other than section then ID (match DISA matrix order)
- Do NOT change the backend — grouping is a frontend concern computed from existing rows

NOT in scope:
- Batch "triage all as..." for duplicate comments (separate card)
- AI-powered duplicate detection (future)
- Keyboard shortcuts for triage decisions (separate card)

title: Backfill request_uuid for non-HTTP audit creation paths
description:
**Surfaced 2026-05-02 by audit-compliance agent review (Q2).**

The audited gem auto-populates `request_uuid` only inside Rails HTTP requests (via `Audited::Sweeper` Rack middleware). Verified: 3492/5126 (68%) of dev DB audits have NULL request_uuid — all `auditable_type='BaseRule'` from seed/import paths.

## Affected paths

- Rake tasks (e.g. `stig_and_srg_puller:pull`)
- Seeds (`db/seeds.rb`)
- ActiveJob workers
- `Component#duplicate_reviews_and_history` (Ruby method, not request-scoped)
- `ReviewBuilder` import path (uses `Review.insert!` so no audit at all)
- `after_commit` / async hooks dispatched after request ends

## Impact

`AuditEventBundle.bundled_with` (commit `7a7fc2e`) returns just the trigger row when request_uuid is nil — forensic correlation breaks for any non-HTTP-driven multi-row operation.

## Fix shape

In `app/lib/vulcan_audit.rb`, add `before_create :backfill_request_uuid_for_jobs` that pulls from `Audited.store` OR a thread-local set by job middleware (e.g. `ActiveJob::Base.around_perform`).

Document the boundary in `post-merge-remediation-notes.md`: "request_uuid correlation requires either an HTTP request or job middleware that sets the thread-local."

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Job middleware sets `Audited.store[:current_request_uuid]` per job
- [ ] Rake-task helper sets it for long-running tasks
- [ ] before_create hook backfills if unset (uses SecureRandom.uuid for orphans, with a flag column or comment indicating "non-request-scoped")
- [ ] Test: audit created in a job has request_uuid
- [ ] Test: audit created in a rake task has request_uuid (with helper)
notes:
Surfaced by audit-compliance agent review on .4 work, 2026-05-02. Closes Q2 forensic correlation gap.
[2026-05-02] Sequencing agent flagged the .4 → 14r edge as false serialization — AuditEventBundle (the .4 component 14r consumes) already shipped in commit 7a7fc2e. 14r is technically parallel-safe with remaining .4 work (F1/F2/F3/F5/F6/F7 don't touch app/lib/vulcan_audit.rb). bd dep remove not available in this bd version; edge remains as a soft block until .4 closes.
close_reason: VulcanAudit invariant + Audited.store hook shipped. Job middleware filed as forward-looking follow-up.

title: move_to_rule writes outbound audit on source rule
description:
**Surfaced 2026-05-02 by DB-schema agent review.**

`reviews_controller.rb:629-635` `move_review_subtree!` writes one audit per moved review (good), and via `vulcan_audited associated_with: :rule` (`review.rb:48`) the audit is attached to the NEW rule. Source rule has no record of an outbound move.

## Forensic asymmetry

- Reviewer auditing destination rule Z: sees "comment Y moved here" ✓
- Reviewer auditing source rule X: sees nothing about Y leaving ✗

## Fix

Before walking subtree, write `action: 'review_moved_out'` audit on source rule referencing destination rule_id + review_id. Mirror the pattern at `reviews_controller.rb:398` (Component-level audit before destroy).

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] move_to_rule writes outbound audit on source rule before walk
- [ ] Audit row carries: source_rule_id, destination_rule_id, review_id, audit_comment
- [ ] Test: source rule's audit feed shows the outbound entry
- [ ] Test: destination rule's audit feed still shows the inbound (existing behavior unchanged)
notes: Surfaced by DB agent review on .4 work, 2026-05-02. Forensic completeness for cross-rule operations.
close_reason: Outbound audit on source rule shipped. 4 new specs, 98/98 reviews_spec green.

title: Two-pass validate_foreign_key for original lifecycle migration FKs
description:
**Surfaced 2026-05-02 by DB-schema agent review.**

`db/migrate/20260429145530_add_lifecycle_columns_to_reviews.rb:24-27` adds 4 FKs (`triage_set_by_id`, `adjudicated_by_id`, `duplicate_of_review_id`, `responding_to_review_id`) inline with column adds. Rails 8 default `add_foreign_key` validates immediately, taking ACCESS EXCLUSIVE on the `reviews` table for the duration of validation. On a populated production reviews table this can lock writes for seconds.

Same anti-pattern as the pre-fix index migration (`.2`) — same Strong Migrations 2-pass remediation.

## Fix

Convert to two-pass per Strong Migrations:
1. Original migration: change to `add_foreign_key … validate: false`
2. New migration: `disable_ddl_transaction!` + `validate_foreign_key :reviews, column: ...` for each of the 4 FKs

Already past initial deploy on `feat/viewer-comments` so this is post-merge cleanup, not blocker.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] All 4 FKs have `validate: false` on `add_foreign_key`
- [ ] New `disable_ddl_transaction!` migration runs `validate_foreign_key` for each
- [ ] Schema.rb identical net state
- [ ] Verified on fresh test DB
notes: Surfaced by DB agent review on .4 work, 2026-05-02. Post-merge cleanup; not blocker.
close_reason: Completed in session B (commit eef28a7 as kea). Lifecycle FKs split into 2-pass pattern.

title: Add zip-bomb decompression budget to json_archive import
description:
**Surfaced 2026-05-02 by security-review agent on `.4` work.** `JsonArchiveImporter` accepts operator-uploaded zip archives. Current controls:

- `100.megabytes` upload cap (`projects_controller.rb:20`) — **pre-decompression only**
- rubyzip 2.4.x `Zip.validate_entry_sizes` defaults true — **per-entry only, not aggregate**
- Whole import wrapped in one `ActiveRecord::Base.transaction` (`json_archive_importer.rb:179`) — DB connection held for entire decompression duration

## Threat

A 50–100 MB JSON-of-empty-arrays archive decompresses to multiple GB before Ruby OOMs. Even non-malicious operator misuse (fat-fingered export of a huge component history) hangs the worker + holds the DB connection.

Trusted-admin model means severity is "noisy worker hang" not "data corruption", but the failure mode is opaque and happens silently until OOM.

## Fix shape

- Iterate archive entries computing `entry.size` (uncompressed) against an aggregate budget (proposed: 500 MB)
- Reject with HTTP 413 + clear toast before parsing any entry
- Add unit spec with a fixture archive that exceeds budget

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `JsonArchiveImporter` enumerates entries, computes total uncompressed size before reading
- [ ] Reject with HTTP 413 + clear error message when over budget
- [ ] Per-archive budget configurable via Settings (default 500 MB)
- [ ] Test: archive exceeding budget rejected before DB transaction opens
- [ ] Test: archive under budget proceeds normally
- [ ] No regression on existing 47 importer specs
notes: Surfaced by security agent review on .4, 2026-05-02. M-effort. Trusted-admin upload context limits severity, but failure mode is opaque/silent.
close_reason: Decompression budget shipped via Settings.import.json_archive_size_budget_mb (default 500 MB). 3 new specs, 51/51 importer specs green.

title: Expand ReviewBlueprint default fields (eliminate frontend refetch)
description:
**Updated 2026-05-02 with cross-validation from Rails-architect + design-review agents on `.4` work.**

`app/blueprints/review_blueprint.rb` default fields = `id, action, comment, created_at, name, triage_status, triage_set_at, adjudicated_at, triager_display_name, triager_imported, adjudicator_display_name, adjudicator_imported` (after `.8` work in commit `fafb71b`).

**Still missing for full frontend self-sufficiency** (per Rails-architect agent Lens 8):
- `rule_id` (CommentTriageModal needs to read it for picker scope)
- `section` (modal renders SectionLabel)
- `responding_to_review_id` (modal needs to know if this is a reply)
- `duplicate_of_review_id` (modal needs to know if this is a duplicate)
- `triage_set_by_id` (forensic queries; today only `triage_set_by_imported_email/name` are exposed for the imported case)
- `author_name` (modal renders blockquote header)
- `author_email` (gated — only when caller is admin tier; mirrors disposition export pattern)

## Why it matters

When triage/adjudicate/section/admin endpoints (`reviews_controller.rb` 8 sites) return `ReviewBlueprint.render_as_hash(@review)`, the modal cannot refresh in place — it must refetch the parent table row via `this.fetch()`. Net: every mutation = 2 round trips.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Default fields expanded to include the 7 missing lifecycle fields
- [ ] author_email gated by `options[:include_email]` in the blueprint (mirror disposition export pattern)
- [ ] Watch ComponentBlueprint default-fields trap (vulcan-component-blueprint-default-fields-trap memory): ensure no Project#available_components.select(...) breaks
- [ ] CommentTriageModal can update its own state from the response payload alone (no `this.fetch()` after triage/adjudicate)
- [ ] Test: ReviewBlueprint.render_as_hash includes all 7 fields
- [ ] Test: author_email present only when include_email: true option passed
- [ ] Test: existing CommentTriageModal vitest specs pass with the richer payload
- [ ] Manual verification: triage a comment via UI, confirm no follow-up `/components/:id/comments` request fires (DevTools Network tab)
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Default fields expanded to include all PR-717 lifecycle fields
- [ ] Watch ComponentBlueprint default-fields trap: ensure no Project#available_components.select(...) breaks
- [ ] Test: ReviewBlueprint.render_as_hash includes triage_status, section, etc.
- [ ] Test: existing ComponentComments tests pass with the richer payload
- [ ] Frontend can drop the post-event refetch (optional follow-up)
notes:

The recursive `move_review_subtree!` at `reviews_controller.rb:618-624` walks N-deep. A bug like `responses.first&.update!(rule_id: ...)` or "only descend one level" would pass the test.

title: Strengthen idempotent section test: target the controller short-circuit explicitly
description: `spec/requests/reviews_spec.rb:1196-1203` idempotent test trivially passes. Pre-condition `update!(section: 'check_content')` runs WITHOUT `audit_comment` set, so audited gem records 1 audit. The PATCH `section: 'check_content', audit_comment: 'noop'` is a no-op. The assertion `audits.count == before_count` would pass even if the controller wrote an audit when input matches current — Rails `update!(same_value)` triggers no Dirty change, no audit. Test catches nothing.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Test rewritten to assert controller short-circuited (e.g., response body contains `{idempotent: true}` if added, or assertion that the audit_comment string is NOT in any audit) — proves the test catches a regression where the controller writes a redundant audit
- [ ] Spec passes
close_reason: Idempotent flag surfaced in response. Spec asserts presence on no-change + absence on real change. RuboCop clean, 10/10 section specs green.

title: Prevent audit-laundering chain (admin_destroy → re-import wipes trail)
description: Combines H2 + H4. After `admin_destroy` (`reviews_controller.rb:373-406`) cascades replies, audited gem keeps audit rows for destroyed records (good). But re-import via `Review.insert!` skips audited entirely — re-imported review has no audit record. Malicious admin can destroy + re-import to launder lifecycle history (no triage_set_by audit, no destroy event tied to resurrected row).
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] On import, write a Component-level audit record listing imported review external_ids with source archive identifier
- [ ] Test: import a fresh archive — Component has audit row `action='import_reviews'` with external_ids list
- [ ] Test: destroy review, export, import — destroyed-then-restored review's history is reconstructible from Component audit
- [ ] Documented in `docs/plans/PR717-public-comment-review/` followup notes
notes:
[2026-05-01 closed in commit 2db6507]
- ReviewBuilder writes Component-level audit row per import: action='import_reviews', audited_changes={archive_vulcan_version, archive_exported_at, review_external_ids}, comment="Imported N reviews from backup archive (vulcan_version=X, exported_at=Y)".
- Audit row only created when ReviewBuilder receives both component: and manifest: kwargs (test/legacy callers without those kwargs skip the audit).
- JsonArchiveImporter accepts imported_by: kwarg, threads it + manifest into ReviewBuilder.
- Tests: 5 unit specs on ReviewBuilder.write_import_audit + 1 integration test via full importer flow.
- Followup notes: docs/plans/PR717-public-comment-review/post-merge-remediation-notes.md (covers .2, .9, .10).
- Reconstruction: union of (a) per-Review destroy audits on originating instance + (b) Component import_reviews row → traces destroyed-then-restored review back to source archive.
- Acceptance: all 4 ACs met (audit row written, external_ids list, source archive identified, documented).
close_reason: Component-level import audit committed in 2db6507. 12/22 cards closed on epic.

title: Add associated_with: :rule to vulcan_audited on Review (audit-trail recoverability)
description: `app/models/review.rb:43` `vulcan_audited only:` lacks `associated_with: :rule`. All other audited models use it (Rule, Membership, AdditionalQuestion, etc.). After `admin_destroy` cascade, audit rows are orphaned — `auditable_id` points to a deleted Review, no `associated_id` to query through. Federal compliance posture: trail exists but is queryable only via raw SQL. Comment at `reviews_controller.rb:372` acknowledges the gap.

title: json_archive: warn instead of silently dropping triage_set_by/adjudicated_by
description:
**Scope expanded 2026-05-01 (Aaron approved):** original card was "warn instead of silently dropping triage_set_by/adjudicated_by". New scope: preserve original attribution per-review when the User can't be resolved on import.

**Why scope grew:** the agent framing ("warn and proceed with nil FK") still loses WHO triaged on cross-instance restore. Federal compliance audit posture requires the attribution chain to survive. Researched GitLab's post-migration mapping (creates placeholder User records + reassignment join table — overkill for Vulcan's one-shot backup/restore use case) and Discourse's external_id pattern (different problem). For Vulcan's scale: 4 columns on Review carry the original email + name when the User doesn't exist on the target.

**Schema** (4 nullable string columns on `reviews`):
- `triage_set_by_imported_email`
- `triage_set_by_imported_name`
- `adjudicated_by_imported_email`
- `adjudicated_by_imported_name`

**Behavior:**
- Resolve User → set FK as today, leave imported_* nil
- Can't resolve → leave FK nil, populate imported_* from archive JSON
- Display: fall back to imported_* with annotation when FK is nil
- Disposition export: same fallback in CSV cells
- Audit: imported_* columns NOT in vulcan_audited only: list (set once at import, never edited)

**References (consulted before deciding):**
- https://docs.gitlab.com/development/user_contribution_mapping/
- https://docs.gitlab.com/user/import/mapping/

**Touch points:**
- New migration: 4 nullable string columns on reviews
- `app/services/import/json_archive/review_builder.rb`: populate imported_* when resolve_user returns nil but archive has email/name
- `app/blueprints/review_blueprint.rb`: expose imported_* fields + computed triager_label / adjudicator_label
- `app/lib/disposition_matrix_export.rb`: fallback in build_row for "Triaged By"/"Adjudicated By"
- `app/javascript/components/components/CommentTriageModal.vue` + `ComponentComments.vue`: render fallback with "imported, no account" annotation
- Tests: importer (3 specs), display (2 specs), export (2 specs)
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Migration adds 4 nullable string cols: triage_set_by_imported_email/name + adjudicated_by_imported_email/name
- [ ] When import resolves user successfully, imported_* cols stay nil
- [ ] When import can't resolve user but archive has email/name, imported_* cols populated and FK left nil
- [ ] When import can't resolve and archive has NO email/name, imported_* cols stay nil (no synthesized data)
- [ ] Display falls back to imported_* with "imported, no account on this instance" annotation
- [ ] Disposition CSV export falls back to imported_* in Triaged By + Adjudicated By cells
- [ ] No regression on existing import specs
- [ ] imported_* cols NOT in vulcan_audited only: list
- [ ] Warning still recorded in import result (carries the email + which review)
notes:
[2026-05-01 backend complete in commit b1ce575]
- Migration: 4 cols added to reviews
- Importer: populates imported_* when User can't resolve, adds warning
- Disposition export: falls back to imported_* in Triaged By + Adjudicated By cells with "(imported, no account: <email>)" annotation
- Tests: 87/87 green (importer 47 + disposition 40)

PENDING for next session:
- ReviewBlueprint: expose imported_* fields + computed triager_label / adjudicator_label
- Vue display: fallback rendering in CommentTriageModal + ComponentComments with "imported, no account" annotation
- Tests: blueprint spec + vitest for the 2 components
[2026-05-01 frontend complete in commit fafb71b]
- Review model: 4 new methods (triager_display_name, triager_imported?, adjudicator_display_name, adjudicator_imported?) — single source of truth for the FK→imported_* fallback.
- ReviewBlueprint: exposes the four + triage_status, triage_set_at, adjudicated_at for the modal.
- Component#paginated_comments + Project#paginated_comments: row hash includes the four display fields so the modal has the data on open (no extra fetch).
- CommentTriageModal.vue: renders "Triaged by ... · time" and "Adjudicated by ... · time" lines conditioned on triage_set_at / adjudicated_at, with a "imported" warning badge when *_imported is true.
- Tests: 14 new on Review model, 6 on ReviewBlueprint (refind: true on the let_it_be to avoid update_columns in-memory cache), 3 on paginated_comments, 6 on CommentTriageModal vitest.
- Visual verification with Playwright on /components/1/triage covering all 4 cases (imported triager only, resolved triager only, imported adjudicator + resolved triager mixed, and the placeholder/empty case). Screenshots in .beads/screenshots/pr717-8-*.png.

ALL acceptance criteria met. Closing.

title: Add project membership check to withdraw + update (security gap)
description: `app/controllers/reviews_controller.rb:10` excludes `withdraw` from `:set_project_from_review` only-list. Combined with line 16 `:authorize_review_owner` (which only checks `@review.user_id == current_user.id`), a user removed from the project (or whose project visibility was revoked) can still withdraw + update their own pending comments. Same for `update`. The comment block at line 17-19 claims `@project is set` by `set_project_from_review` — that's false for `withdraw`.

title: Inherited-requirements as first-class workflow (PR #717 Task 32)
description:
DISA Vendor STIG Process v4r1 §4.1.15 prescribes the inheritance pattern as: status=Applicable-Does Not Meet + Mitigation field with canonical sentence "This requirement is fully mitigated by [parent-STIG-RuleID]" + Status Justification. Vulcan today writes to the wrong field (vendor_comments), picks the wrong status (auto-overrides to Configurable), and uses internal SV-ids instead of human-readable STIG-IDs. Authors have no UI button for "Mark as Inherited"; commenters get no badge.

Schema:
- base_rules.inherited_external_citation (string)
- base_rules.inheritance_justification (text)

Model:
- Rule#inherited? (satisfied_by.any? || external citation present)
- Validator: inherited → status must be DNM
- Validator: inherited → justification required
- Drop status auto-override at rule.rb:140
- New Rule#mitigation_export_text helper

Export:
- Spreadsheet: Mitigation column carries citation; Status Justification carries justification
- XCCDF: verify DISA placement (likely vendor-specific extension)
- Disposition CSV (Task 29): add Status + Mitigation columns

UI:
- "Mark as Inherited" button + modal (two tabs: from-Vulcan-rule cross-project picker / from-external-STIG)
- Justification textarea required, min 50 chars
- Inherited badge on rule list/editor
- Commenter view: badge + hint "consider commenting on the parent canonical"

PR-717 integration:
- Mark-as-duplicate picker (Task 24): suggest parent canonical when current rule is inherited
- Disposition CSV: add Status + Mitigation columns

Acceptance:
- [ ] Migration adds the two columns
- [ ] Inherited rule with status != DNM is invalid
- [ ] Inherited rule with blank justification is invalid
- [ ] Spreadsheet export emits canonical Mitigation sentence
- [ ] XCCDF export places Mitigation correctly
- [ ] Cross-project parent rule picker works
- [ ] Existing satisfied_by data untouched on migration (no surprise changes)
- [ ] Disposition CSV (Task 29) has Status + Mitigation columns
- [ ] PR-717 commenter UI shows Inherited badge
- [ ] All tests green
close_reason: Replaced by docs/plans/PR717-public-comment-review/31-inherited-requirements-workflow.md plan file. Per vulcan-comments-as-objects memory, plan files are the canonical PR-717 tracking; bd cards fragment context. Aaron's call (2026-04-30): Task 31 is FOLLOW-UP phase, not in this PR.

These need separate migration before the overrides can be deleted.

title: H4: Batch access_request lookup in ProjectsController#index
description:
**Location:** `app/controllers/projects_controller.rb:24-33`

**Problem:** Per-project `current_user.access_requests.find_by(project_id:)` is N+1. 50 projects = 50 queries.

**Fix:** Batch: `ar_by_project = current_user.access_requests.where(project_id: project_ids).index_by(&:project_id)` then hash lookup.

**Depends on:** Nothing — standalone controller fix.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] access_request_id uses batch hash lookup, not per-project find_by
- [ ] Test: 10 projects generates 1 access_request query (not 10)
- [ ] Existing projects specs pass
- [ ] TDD red -> green

title: H5: Add eager_load to ComponentsController#find rule serialization
description:
**Location:** `app/controllers/components_controller.rb:426`

**Problem:** `render json: rules` triggers full `Rule#as_json` without eager loading associations (reviews, satisfies, satisfied_by, srg_rule). After C3 is fixed, the SRG N+1 is gone, but the association N+1 remains.

**Fix:** Add `.eager_load(:reviews, :satisfies, :satisfied_by, :srg_rule)` to the rules query, or use `as_json(skip_merge: true)` for search results that don't need full detail.

**Depends on:** C3 (Rule#as_json fix).
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Component find action eager-loads rule associations
- [ ] Test: find with 10 matching rules generates < 10 queries (not 50+)
- [ ] Existing component find specs pass
- [ ] TDD red -> green

title: H2: Add .limit() to unbounded user audit query in UsersController#index
description:
**Location:** `app/controllers/users_controller.rb:15-18`

**Problem:** `Audited.audit_class.where(auditable_type: 'User').map(&:format)` loads ALL user audit records without `.limit()`. Grows unbounded over time. On a mature instance, thousands of records materialized into Ruby.

**Fix:** Add `.limit(200)` before `.map(&:format)`.

**Depends on:** Nothing — standalone fix.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Audit query has .limit(200) or similar
- [ ] Test: with 500 audit records, only 200 returned
- [ ] Existing users_controller specs pass
- [ ] TDD red -> green
notes: [2026-04-07] Still needs .limit() on users_controller audit query. Not addressed by Blueprinter migration.
close_reason: Fixed: added .limit(200) to audit query in UsersController#index. Test added.

title: H3: Consolidate Project#details from 9 COUNT queries to 1-2
description:
**Location:** `app/models/project.rb:62-73`

**Problem:** 9 separate `rules.where(status: ...).size` queries. Each goes through `has_many :rules, through: :components` join. Called on every project show page.

**Fix:** Single query: `counts = rules.group(:status).count` for status counts, plus `rules.where(locked: false).where(review_requestor_id: nil).count` etc. Reduces 9 queries to 2-3.

**Depends on:** Nothing — standalone model fix.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Project#details uses GROUP BY instead of 9 separate queries
- [ ] Test: details returns same values as before (regression)
- [ ] Test: only 1-3 SQL queries generated (not 9)
- [ ] Project show page specs pass
- [ ] TDD red -> green
notes: [2026-04-07] Still needs GROUP BY consolidation. Not addressed by Blueprinter migration.
close_reason: Fixed: Project#details rewritten with group(:status).count + group(:locked).count + CASE WHEN for review counts. 9 queries → 3. Test verifies ≤4 queries.

title: H1: Optimize check_access_request_notifications (runs every request)
description:
**Location:** `app/controllers/application_controller.rb:268-277`

**Problem:** `before_action` runs on EVERY request. Iterates all available projects, calls `can_admin_project?` per project (membership query each), loads access_requests with eager_load per admin project. For admin with 50 projects = 100+ queries before ANY page renders. Also runs on JSON API calls that don't even render the navbar.

**Fix:**
1. Skip for JSON format: `return @access_requests if request.format.json?`
2. Single query: `ProjectAccessRequest.joins(:project => :memberships).where(memberships: { user_id: current_user.id, role: 'admin' }).eager_load(:user, :project)`
3. Or cache result in session with short TTL

**Depends on:** Nothing — standalone fix in ApplicationController.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Does NOT run N+1 per project
- [ ] Skips for JSON format requests
- [ ] Uses single query instead of iterating projects
- [ ] Test: admin with 10 projects generates < 5 queries (not 20+)
- [ ] Test: JSON API requests don't trigger notification check
- [ ] All request specs pass
- [ ] TDD red -> green
close_reason: Closed

title: Restore prev_unconfirmed_email capture for reconfirmation flash message
description:
**Location:** `app/controllers/users/registrations_controller.rb:29`

**Buggy code:**
```ruby
resource.unconfirmed_email if resource.respond_to?(:unconfirmed_email)
```

**Problem:** This is a no-op — reads `unconfirmed_email` and throws away the value. Compare to stock Devise which does:
```ruby
prev_unconfirmed_email = resource.unconfirmed_email if resource.respond_to?(:unconfirmed_email)
```
and then passes `prev_unconfirmed_email` to `set_flash_message_for_update(resource, prev_unconfirmed_email)` to tell the user "We sent a confirmation link to your new email address" vs "Profile updated successfully."

**Fix:** Either capture to a local and use it for flash messaging, or delete the line entirely if the simplified flash is intentional.

**Status:** NOT yet fixed.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Request spec: user changes email → flash says 'confirmation link sent to new address' (not generic 'profile updated')
- [ ] Request spec: user does not change email → flash says 'profile updated'
- [ ] TDD red → green
- [ ] Cross-check with Devise stock RegistrationsController#update to match behavior
close_reason: Closed

title: Fix Slack notification firing on every user update instead of only admin changes
description:
**Location:** `app/controllers/users_controller.rb:70-72`

**Buggy code:**
```ruby
if @user.update(user_update_params)
  notification_type = @user.admin ? :assign_vulcan_admin : :remove_vulcan_admin
  send_slack_notification(notification_type, @user) if Settings.slack.enabled
```

**Problem:** Every successful user update fires a Slack notification as either "assign_vulcan_admin" or "remove_vulcan_admin" — regardless of whether the admin flag actually changed. A simple name change broadcasts "User demoted from vulcan admin" to Slack.

**Fix:** Gate on `@user.saved_change_to_admin?`.

**Status:** Fix applied in working tree. Tests NOT yet written.

**Why:** Slack spam damages trust in notifications; false "promoted/demoted" messages confuse ops team.
**How to apply:** Only notify when admin flag actually changed, not on every update. TDD required.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Request spec: update name only → no Slack call
- [ ] Request spec: update email only → no Slack call
- [ ] Request spec: toggle admin to true → :assign_vulcan_admin called
- [ ] Request spec: toggle admin to false → :remove_vulcan_admin called
- [ ] Request spec: update name + toggle admin → exactly one Slack call (admin change)
- [ ] TDD red → green
- [ ] No regressions in users_controller_spec
close_reason: Closed

title: Fix polymorphic audit query missing user_type filter in registrations#edit
description:
**Location:** `app/controllers/users/registrations_controller.rb:11-12`

**Buggy code:**
```ruby
@histories = Audited.audit_class.includes(:user)
                    .where(user_id: current_user.id)
```

**Problem:** `Audited::Audit#user` is a polymorphic association (`user_id` + `user_type` columns). Filtering on `user_id` alone is incorrect for polymorphic data. Today every actor is a User so IDs don't collide, but `VulcanAudit.create_initial_rule_audit_from_mapping` already uses `user_type: 'System'`, which this query would leak if System shared an ID with current_user.

**Fix:** Add `user_type: 'User'` to the where clause.

**Status:** Fix applied in working tree. Tests NOT yet written.

**Why:** Latent bug — breaks the moment any non-User actor shares an ID space with a User.
**How to apply:** TDD: create an audit with user_type='System' and same id; verify it's excluded from results.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Request spec: create audit with same user_id but user_type='System' → NOT returned in @histories
- [ ] Request spec: create audit with user_type='User' → returned in @histories
- [ ] TDD red → green
- [ ] users_controller#index has same pattern — verify already correct (filters by auditable_type)
close_reason: Closed

title: Switch XLSX export from FastExcel to caxlsx for xml:space=preserve whitespace fidelity
description:
## Problem
FastExcel writes cells via `write_string()` — plain text only, no `xml:space="preserve"` attribute on `<t>` elements in the shared strings table. When Excel re-saves, whitespace in multiline markdown content (code blocks, indentation, blank lines) gets normalized, causing false-positive diffs on re-import (user edits 1 rule, system detects 9 changes).

## Root Cause
Research confirmed: the issue is NOT Excel modifying content, but the XLSX writer failing to mark whitespace for preservation. `xml:space="preserve"` on `<t>` elements tells XML parsers to keep all whitespace as-is.

## Solution
Switch from FastExcel to **caxlsx** (community axlsx) which:
- Sets `xml:space="preserve"` by default on shared strings
- Supports `use_shared_strings = true` for multiline content
- Supports `wrap_text: true` cell styling
- Supports data validation dropdowns (future: vulcan-clean-di3)

## Key Files
- `app/services/export/formatters/excel_formatter.rb` — current FastExcel writer
- `Gemfile` — swap gem dependency

## Research Sources
- caxlsx defaults `xml_space = :preserve` in SharedStringsTable
- FastExcel has no rich text or whitespace preservation support
- Excel, LibreOffice, Google Sheets all honor `xml:space="preserve"`
- Roo `_x000D_` conversion may double newlines from Windows Excel (test)
- No STIG ecosystem tool has solved this — Vulcan would be first
notes: [2026-02-22 11:30] Completed: Switched FastExcel → caxlsx gem. Added Source column (Direct/Inherited) to Excel exports with grey fill, locked cells, dropdowns (Status/Severity/Source), sheet protection, auto-filter. Fixed non-deterministic satisfied_by.first → satisfied_by.order(:id).first in export_checktext/export_fixtext. 343 tests pass, 0 failures. Live tested: real edit detected correctly. 8 inherited-row false positives still appear due to compute_rule_changes using csv_attributes which hits the same non-deterministic path at runtime. Next: consider skipping check/fix comparison for inherited rows in compute_rule_changes, OR live test again after .order(:id) fix applied to rule.rb.
close_reason: caxlsx swap complete with Source column, styling, dropdowns, sheet protection. 343 tests pass.

description:

title: Schema validation for XCCDF (XSD), JSON Archive, CSV imports
description:
Add schema validation for structured file imports.

## Work Items

1. **XCCDF: Validate against official XSD** — XCCDF 1.2 has a NIST XSD schema. Use `Nokogiri::XML::Schema` to validate uploads before processing. Download/vendor the XSD from NIST SCAP repo.

2. **JSON Archive: Strict manifest schema** — Validate manifest.json structure before import:
   - backup_format_version in supported versions
   - components array with max item count (e.g., 100)
   - Each component: name (string, max 255), prefix (pattern), srg_id (string)
   - Rule data: validate locked_fields keys against LOCKABLE_SECTION_NAMES
   - Review action validated against allowed enum

3. **CSV formula injection sanitization** — Strip leading `=`, `+`, `-`, `@` from cell values on import. These execute as formulas when exported CSVs are opened in Excel by DISA reviewers.

4. **Zip bomb protection** — Check uncompressed entry sizes before reading from ZIP (JSON Archive + XLSX). Set max total decompressed size (e.g., 500 MB).

## Files
- app/lib/xccdf/ (XSD validation)
- app/services/import/json_archive/ (manifest + rule schema)
- app/models/component.rb (CSV sanitization in from_spreadsheet)
- db/seeds/schemas/ (vendor the XCCDF XSD)

## Tests
- Spec: invalid XCCDF fails XSD validation with clear error
- Spec: malformed manifest rejected with specific field errors
- Spec: formula-prefixed cell values are sanitized
- Spec: zip bomb detected and rejected
notes: DISA STIGs use XCCDF 1.1 (not 1.2). Schema: xccdf-1.1.4.xsd. Already available at ../cis-bench/schemas/xccdf-1.1.4.xsd plus dependencies (cpe, simpledc, xml.xsd, platform). Vendor these into db/seeds/schemas/ or lib/schemas/. Both 1.1 and 1.2 XSDs available if needed.

title: Per-section lock UX: field state visualization
description:
## Problem
Section-locked fields look identical to whole-rule-locked and under-review fields — all just grey/disabled. Users can't tell WHY a field is disabled.

## Solution
Add distinct colored left-border indicators to form groups:
- **Yellow** (`#ffc107`) — section locked by reviewer
- **Blue** (`#17a2b8`) — under review, awaiting approval  
- **Grey** (`#6c757d`) — whole-rule locked via review system
- **Default** — editable

Add a small legend component above the form when any non-default state is active.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Section-locked fields have yellow left border + lock icon
- [ ] Under-review fields have blue left border
- [ ] Whole-rule-locked fields have grey left border (existing disabled look)
- [ ] Legend shows active states with color key
- [ ] Visual states apply to RuleForm, DisaRuleDescriptionForm, CheckForm
- [ ] Mount tests verify correct CSS classes per state
- [ ] All 1807+ frontend tests pass

title: Bug: DISA Excel multi-component export produces blank worksheets
description: When exporting multiple components via DISA Excel (VendorSubmission + Excel), the worksheets in the resulting .xlsx are blank despite correct worksheet names. Single-component exports work fine. The issue is likely in ExcelFormatter.generate_workbook or how FastExcel handles constant_memory mode with multiple sheets. Debug by comparing single vs multi-component paths in Export::Base.export_as_workbook. The old ExportHelper.export_excel works correctly for multi-component — diff the two approaches.
close_reason: Fixed: ExcelFormatter called read_string before close in constant_memory mode. Swapping order fixed all blank worksheet exports.

The export system has 8 known gaps (docs/disa-process/export-requirements.md) and 4 planned export modes (Vendor Submission, Published STIG, Working Copy, Confidential/CUI). Testing the current broken exports would encode bugs as requirements.

Writing tests against current output means rewriting every test after those cards land.

title: CSV header alignment: export headers != import headers
description:
STIG/SRG CSV export uses `BENCHMARK_CSV_COLUMNS` headers:
- `Title`, `Description`, `Check`, `Fix`, `STIG ID`, `SRG ID`, etc.

Component spreadsheet import expects `IMPORT_MAPPING` headers:
- `Requirement`, `VulDiscussion`, `Check`, `Fix`, `STIGID`, `SRGID`, etc.

Header names don't align, so you can't export a STIG CSV and import it as a Component without renaming columns manually.

## Approach Options
1. **Make import accept both header sets** — import recognizes aliases (e.g., `Title` OR `Requirement`)
2. **Add Component CSV export with import-compatible headers** — separate "round-trip" format
3. **Align all headers to one standard** — breaking change for existing users

Option 1 is safest (Postel's Law again — liberal import).

## Tests
- Round-trip test: export Component CSV → re-import as new Component → verify field fidelity
- Import with BENCHMARK_CSV_COLUMNS headers → should succeed
- Import with IMPORT_MAPPING headers → should still succeed (backwards compat)
close_reason: Already fixed in commit f54d67a. HEADER_ALIASES in import_constants.rb + normalize_import_headers in component.rb. Tests at components_spec.rb:125 (aliases) and :525 (round-trip).
