title: Search quality testing: ambiguity, common patterns, fuzzing
description:
## Problem
Global search returns results from 7 categories (Projects, Components, Rules, SRGs, STIGs, STIG Rules, SRG Rules). Users may click wrong category when same term appears in multiple places.

Example: Search "CIS" might match:
- Component: "CIS Benchmark for RHEL 9" (user's project)
- STIG: "CIS Controls Reference" (published doc)

User clicks wrong one → confusion.

## Test Gaps Identified

### 1. Ambiguity Tests (same name, multiple categories)
- Search "RHEL" with RHEL Component AND RHEL STIG → verify both appear in correct sections
- Search "Windows" with Windows Component AND Windows STIG → verify routing works
- Verify UI clearly distinguishes categories

### 2. Common Security Pattern Tests
- `/etc/ssh/sshd_config` → should find STIG rules with check content
- `CCI-000366` → should find rules with that CCI
- `password complexity` → should find relevant rules
- `audit log` → common security term
- `firewall` → matches many rules

### 3. Fuzzing/Edge Cases
- Very long queries (500+ chars)
- Special characters: `&`, `<`, `>`, quotes, backticks
- SQL injection attempts: `'; DROP TABLE--`
- XSS attempts: `<script>alert(1)</script>`
- Unicode characters
- Empty/whitespace queries

### 4. Ranking/Relevance (future)
- Currently no relevance ranking - results in DB order
- Consider: exact match > prefix match > contains match

## Existing Test Coverage
- `spec/requests/api/search_spec.rb` - 40+ tests covering basic functionality
- `spec/services/search_query_service_spec.rb` - query transformation
- `spec/services/search_abbreviation_service_spec.rb` - abbreviation expansion
- `spec/models/rule_search_spec.rb` - pg_search scopes

## Files to Modify
- `spec/requests/api/search_spec.rb` - add ambiguity and fuzzing tests
- Possibly `app/controllers/api/search_controller.rb` - if issues found

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Add ambiguity tests for overlapping names across categories
- [ ] Add common security pattern tests
- [ ] Add fuzzing/edge case tests
- [ ] All tests pass
- [ ] Document any bugs found
close_reason: Closed

title: Unify controls page layout with Composition API
description:
## Objective
Unify the controls display between `/components/:id` (overview) and `/components/:id/controls` (edit) using Vue 2.7 Composition API and composables, following TDD.

## Architecture

### New Components (Composition API)
1. **ControlsPageLayout.vue** - Shared three-column layout with slots
2. **RuleCommandBar.vue** - Action buttons, extracted from RulesCodeEditorView

### New Composables
1. **useRuleFilters.js** - Filter state & toggle logic
2. **useRuleSelection.js** - Selected rule management (replaces SelectedRulesMixin)
3. **useSidebar.js** - Sidebar open/close state
4. **useRuleActions.js** - Save, lock, review, clone, delete actions

### Existing Components (Keep Options API)
- RuleNavigator.vue - Left sidebar
- RuleFilterBar.vue - Filter switches
- RuleForm.vue - Documentation form
- RuleEditor.vue - Tab container
- Sidebar components (RuleSatisfactions, RuleReviews, RuleHistories)

## Implementation Order (TDD)

### Phase 1: Composables
1. Write tests for useRuleSelection composable
2. Implement useRuleSelection
3. Write tests for useRuleFilters composable
4. Implement useRuleFilters
5. Write tests for useSidebar composable
6. Implement useSidebar
7. Write tests for useRuleActions composable
8. Implement useRuleActions

### Phase 2: Layout Component
1. Write tests for ControlsPageLayout
2. Implement ControlsPageLayout with slots
3. Write tests for RuleCommandBar
4. Implement RuleCommandBar

### Phase 3: Integration
1. Refactor RulesCodeEditorView to use new layout + composables
2. Refactor RulesReadOnlyView to use same layout with readOnly=true
3. Integration tests for both views

### Phase 4: Cleanup
1. Remove SelectedRulesMixin (replaced by composable)
2. Remove duplicate code
3. Fix command bar responsive layout issue

## File Structure

```
app/javascript/
├── components/
│   └── rules/
│       ├── ControlsPageLayout.vue (new)
│       ├── RuleCommandBar.vue (new)
│       ├── RulesCodeEditorView.vue (refactor)
│       └── RulesReadOnlyView.vue (refactor)
├── composables/
│   ├── useRuleFilters.js (new)
│   ├── useRuleSelection.js (new)
│   ├── useSidebar.js (new)
│   └── useRuleActions.js (new)
└── __tests__/
    ├── composables/
    │   ├── useRuleFilters.spec.js
    │   ├── useRuleSelection.spec.js
    │   ├── useSidebar.spec.js
    │   └── useRuleActions.spec.js
    └── components/
        ├── ControlsPageLayout.spec.js
        └── RuleCommandBar.spec.js
```

## Props for ControlsPageLayout

```javascript
props: {
  readOnly: Boolean,        // Disable editing
  showCommandBar: Boolean,  // Show/hide command bar
  showFilterBar: Boolean,   // Show/hide filter bar
}
```

## Slots for ControlsPageLayout

- `header` - Breadcrumb and title area
- `command-bar` - Action buttons (optional)
- `filter-bar` - Filter switches (optional)
- `left-sidebar` - RuleNavigator
- `main-content` - RuleEditor/RuleForm
- `right-panels` - Sidebar slideovers

## Testing Strategy

- **Composables**: Pure function tests with @vue/test-utils
- **Components**: Mount tests with slot injection
- **Integration**: Full page render with mocked API

## Success Criteria

1. Both views share same layout component
2. All composables have 100% test coverage
3. Command bar responsive issue fixed
4. No regression in existing functionality
5. Code ready for Vue 3 migration

title: Fix Remember Me checkbox on login page
description:
## Bug Report
"Remember Me" checkbox on login page does not work correctly.

## Investigation Findings

### What's configured:
- User model includes `:rememberable` devise module ✓
- Login form has `f.check_box :remember_me` ✓
- Default `remember_for = 2.weeks` (not explicitly set, using default) ✓
- `expire_all_remember_me_on_sign_out = true` is set

### Possible causes to investigate:
1. **Secure cookies issue** - If app served over HTTP but cookies marked secure
2. **Timeoutable module** - User model has `:timeoutable` which may override remember me
3. **Session expiration** - Check if session timeout is shorter than remember period
4. **Cookie domain/path** - May not be set correctly for the environment
5. **Browser-specific** - Some browsers block third-party cookies

### Files to check:
- `config/initializers/devise.rb` - line 137 (remember_for), line 140 (expire on sign out)
- `app/models/user.rb` - line 6 (timeoutable), line 12 (rememberable)
- `app/views/devise/sessions/_local.html.haml` - checkbox implementation

### Likely fix:
Check if `:timeoutable` timeout is shorter than remember period, or if running on HTTP with secure cookie settings.
close_reason: Fixed in f180b34 - OmniAuth controller now calls remember_me(user)

title: Auth: Don't merge accounts by email - keep providers separate
description:
Current behavior: When user logs in via OIDC/LDAP with same email as existing local account, provider is silently overwritten, destroying local login capability.

Expected: Each provider+email should be a separate identity, or at minimum warn user before merging.

Found during v2.2.2 testing when local admin account was converted to OIDC account.

See app/models/user.rb:86-104 create_or_update_user_from_auth method.
close_reason: Implemented ProviderConflictError. Existing accounts with different provider now blocked from hijacking. 61 auth tests pass.

title: Docker env defaults and admin bootstrap
description:
## Problem

Docker deployments require `.env` file to exist, breaking "works out of box" experience.
`docker compose up` and `vulcan build --info` fail without `.env`.

## Decisions Made

### 1. Dockerfile Core Defaults
Bake sensible defaults into image so it starts without any config:

```dockerfile
ENV RAILS_ENV="production" \
    RAILS_SERVE_STATIC_FILES="true" \
    RAILS_LOG_TO_STDOUT="true" \
    RAILS_FORCE_SSL="false" \
    PORT=3000 \
    POSTGRES_USER=postgres \
    POSTGRES_PASSWORD=postgres \
    POSTGRES_DB=vulcan_postgres_production \
    DATABASE_PORT=5432 \
    VULCAN_ENABLE_LOCAL_LOGIN="true" \
    VULCAN_ENABLE_OIDC="false" \
    VULCAN_ENABLE_LDAP="false" \
    VULCAN_FIRST_USER_ADMIN="true"
```

### 2. docker-compose.yml
Make `.env` optional (override mechanism, not requirement):

```yaml
env_file:
  - path: .env
    required: false
```

### 3. Admin Bootstrap (Priority Order)

1. **Explicit admin via env vars** (highest priority):
   - `VULCAN_ADMIN_EMAIL` + `VULCAN_ADMIN_PASSWORD`
   - Created on db:prepare if no admin exists

2. **First user becomes admin** (default behavior):
   - `VULCAN_FIRST_USER_ADMIN=true` (default)
   - First login (local/OIDC/OAuth/LDAP) gets admin
   - Makes app functional immediately

3. **Manual admin** (opt-out):
   - Set `VULCAN_FIRST_USER_ADMIN=false`
   - Use rails console to create admin

### 4. Deployment Targets
- Docker Compose: uses .env for overrides
- Helm/K8s: ConfigMaps/Secrets override Dockerfile defaults
- Heroku: `heroku config:set` overrides defaults

## Still To Decide
- Password generation for explicit admin (if VULCAN_ADMIN_PASSWORD not set)
- Password reset mechanisms
- Applies to both v2.2.x and v2.3.0

## Files to Modify
- Dockerfile (both repos)
- docker-compose.yml (both repos)
- db/seeds.rb or new rake task
- app/models/user.rb (omniauth callback)
- ENVIRONMENT_VARIABLES.md
close_reason: Done — commits dcb296d (config defaults aligned), e3e6b20 (New Project button fix), admin bootstrap already existed. Settings docs in docs/getting-started/configuration.md

comments.author: Aaron Lippold
comments.text:
## Database Password Standardization (Added)

### Problem
Two different env vars for database password:
- `POSTGRES_PASSWORD` - used by docker-compose for postgres container
- `VULCAN_VUE_DATABASE_PASSWORD` - used by Rails database.yml (legacy)

This caused confusion and potential misconfiguration.

### Decision
Standardize on 12-factor pattern:
1. `DATABASE_URL` takes precedence (Heroku, K8s, docker-compose)
2. Fallback to individual vars: `POSTGRES_USER`, `POSTGRES_PASSWORD`, `POSTGRES_DB`
3. `VULCAN_VUE_DATABASE_PASSWORD` deprecated but still supported for backward compatibility

### Files Changed
- `config/database.yml` - Added DATABASE_URL support with fallback chain
- `ENVIRONMENT_VARIABLES.md` - Updated database section, deprecation note
- `docs/getting-started/environment-variables.md` - Same

### New database.yml production section
```yaml
production:
  <<: *default
  url: <%= ENV['DATABASE_URL'] %>
  host: <%= ENV.fetch('DATABASE_HOST', 'localhost') %>
  port: <%= ENV.fetch('DATABASE_PORT', 5432) %>
  database: <%= ENV.fetch('POSTGRES_DB', 'vulcan_postgres_production') %>
  username: <%= ENV.fetch('POSTGRES_USER', 'postgres') %>
  password: <%= ENV['POSTGRES_PASSWORD'] || ENV['VULCAN_VUE_DATABASE_PASSWORD'] %>
```

comments.author: Aaron Lippold
comments.text:
## Revised Implementation Plan (Based on Research)

### Research Findings
- No major Rails app (Discourse, GitLab, Mastodon) uses auto-first-user-admin
- WordPress installer race condition attacks are documented and real
- All major apps use explicit CLI/rake tasks for admin bootstrap
- Race conditions need DB-level protection (advisory locks)

### Hybrid Approach (Priority Order)

1. **VULCAN_ADMIN_EMAIL + VULCAN_ADMIN_PASSWORD** (Primary - Most Secure)
   - Runs on db:prepare/startup
   - Explicit, auditable, no race condition
   - Works for Docker, K8s, Heroku

2. **VULCAN_FIRST_USER_ADMIN=true** (Fallback - Convenience)
   - First user becomes admin WITH advisory lock
   - Prevents race condition vulnerability
   - Good for demos/quick evaluations

3. **rake db:create_admin** (Manual - Existing)
   - Keep for backward compatibility

### Implementation Phases

**Phase 1: Env Var Bootstrap**
- New rake task or initializer
- Creates admin from env vars on startup
- Idempotent (skips if admin exists)
- TDD: Write tests first

**Phase 2: First-User-Admin with Advisory Lock**
- Wrap after_create in advisory lock
- Prevents race condition
- TDD: Write tests first

**Phase 3: Integration Testing**
- Test priority order
- Test concurrent registration (race condition prevented)
- Full test suite passes

**Phase 4: Documentation**
- Update ENVIRONMENT_VARIABLES.md
- Update docs/getting-started/
- Update docker-compose examples
- Update .env.example files

title: Unify password complexity requirements across frontend/backend
description:
## Problem
Password complexity requirements are currently:
- Hardcoded in frontend (PasswordInput.vue)
- Potentially different in backend (Devise validation)
- Not configurable per deployment
- User reported they're "not in sync throughout the app"

## Current Frontend Requirements (PasswordInput.vue)
```
• At least 8 characters (12+ recommended)
• Uppercase and lowercase letters
• Numbers and special characters
```

## What Needs to Happen

1. **Backend Configuration** (Single Source of Truth)
   - Define requirements in Settings/ENV (min_length, require_uppercase, etc.)
   - Implement Devise custom validator using these settings
   - Expose via API endpoint: GET /api/auth/password_requirements

2. **Frontend Reads from Backend**
   - PasswordInput.vue fetches requirements from API
   - Dynamically displays rules based on backend config
   - Strength calculator uses backend rules

3. **Configurable Per Deployment**
   - Add to config/vulcan.default.yml
   - ENV vars: VULCAN_PASSWORD_MIN_LENGTH, etc.
   - Different deployments can set different policies

## Files to Check/Modify
- `app/javascript/components/auth/PasswordInput.vue` (hardcoded rules)
- `app/models/user.rb` (Devise validations)
- `config/vulcan.default.yml` (add password policy settings)
- `app/controllers/api/auth/password_requirements_controller.rb` (new - expose config)

## References
- User feedback: "password complexity system not in sync"
- Current implementation: lines 35-66 in PasswordInput.vue
notes:
[2026-02-19] Research complete. Current state:
- Backend: Devise `:validatable` = 6-128 chars only, no complexity
- Frontend: PasswordField.vue = display wrapper only, zero validation
- No password settings in vulcan.default.yml
- HAML views show "(6 characters minimum)" hint only

Plan for next session:
1. Add `vuelidate@0.7.7` (Vue 2 compatible, BootstrapVue recommended)
2. Add password policy to vulcan.default.yml (min_length, require_uppercase, require_lowercase, require_number, require_special)
3. Create PasswordPolicyValidator (custom Devise validator reading Settings)
4. Expose policy via Settings.password (already available in HAML layout)
5. Enhance PasswordField.vue with real-time validation using Vuelidate
6. TDD: write specs first (model validation, request spec, Vitest component)
7. Use let_it_be for expensive setup, @test/testHelper for DRY test infra

Key files:
- app/models/user.rb (add custom validator)
- config/initializers/devise.rb (password_length from Settings)
- config/vulcan.default.yml (password policy section)
- app/javascript/components/shared/PasswordField.vue (add Vuelidate)
- app/views/devise/registrations/_form.html.haml (pass policy to Vue)
- app/views/devise/passwords/edit.html.haml (pass policy to Vue)
close_reason: Password policy unification complete: count-based DoD 2222 defaults, backend validator, frontend checklist, docs

title: Migrate Login/Auth to SPA with TDD
description:
# Migrate Login/Auth to SPA with TDD

## Problem
- Current server-rendered login has test failures (redirect loops in test environment)
- Login2.vue exists but doesn't fit our design system
- Mixed server/client authentication creates complexity

## Solution
Migrate authentication to SPA using established pattern (API → Store → Composable → Page).

## Design Direction (Based on Research)

### Layout Pattern (Inspired by NuxtUI AuthForm)
**Vertical Stack Layout:**
1. **Header**: Logo + "Sign in to Vulcan" title
2. **Provider Buttons**: OIDC, GitHub, LDAP (full-width, stacked)
3. **Divider**: "or continue with email" separator
4. **Form**: Email/password with floating labels (Bootstrap 5 pattern)
5. **Submit**: Full-width "Sign In" button
6. **Footer**: Links (forgot password, etc.)

### Bootstrap 5 Styling
- **Floating labels** for modern form UX
- **btn-outline-secondary** for provider buttons with brand icons
- **btn-primary** for submit button
- **alert-danger** for validation errors
- **Responsive** card layout (max-w-md equivalent)
- **Clean, minimal** design matching existing SPA pages

### Component Structure
```
LoginPage.vue (page wrapper)
├─ AuthLayout.vue (centered card layout)
   ├─ LoginHeader.vue (logo + title)
   ├─ AuthProviderButtons.vue (OIDC/GitHub/LDAP)
   ├─ Divider.vue ("or" separator)
   ├─ LoginForm.vue (email/password with floating labels)
   └─ LoginFooter.vue (links)
```

### Key Features
- Loading states on buttons during authentication
- Real-time validation feedback
- Keyboard navigation support (Enter to submit)
- Accessible (proper labels, ARIA attributes)
- Mobile-optimized touch targets
- Matches Bootstrap 5 design system used in SPA

## Architecture (TDD)

### 1. Backend API (Already Exists)
SessionsController#create already has JSON support:
```ruby
format.json do
  self.resource = warden.authenticate!(auth_options)
  sign_in(resource_name, resource)
  render json: { user: resource.as_json(only: %i[id email admin]) }, status: :ok
end
```

**Add Tests:**
- Request specs for JSON login flow
- Error cases: invalid credentials, locked account, etc.
- Multiple providers: local, OIDC, GitHub, LDAP

### 2. API Layer
`app/javascript/apis/sessions.ts`
```typescript
export const sessionsApi = {
  login: (email: string, password: string) => 
    axios.post('/users/sign_in.json', { user: { email, password } }),
  logout: () => 
    axios.delete('/users/sign_out.json'),
  currentUser: () => 
    axios.get('/api/current_user.json')
}
```

**Tests:** Mock HTTP responses, error handling

### 3. Pinia Store
`app/javascript/stores/auth.store.ts`
- State: currentUser, loading, error
- Actions: login, logout, checkAuth
- Getters: isAuthenticated, isAdmin

**Tests:** Store actions, mutations, getters

### 4. Composable
`app/javascript/composables/useAuth.ts`
- Wraps auth store
- Business logic for login/logout
- Error formatting
- Redirect handling

**Tests:** Login flow, error states, redirects

### 5. Page & Components
`app/javascript/pages/auth/LoginPage.vue`
- Bootstrap 5 design (NOT Login2.vue style)
- Supports all auth providers
- Loading states, error messages
- Responsive design

**Components:**
- `LoginForm.vue` - Email/password form
- `AuthProviderButtons.vue` - OIDC/GitHub/LDAP buttons
- `AuthLayout.vue` - Shared layout for auth pages

**Tests:** Component tests for each

### 6. Router Integration
Update Vue Router with `/login` route
Guard for authenticated routes
Redirect after login

## Success Criteria
- [ ] All tests pass (backend + frontend)
- [ ] Login works with all providers (local, OIDC, GitHub, LDAP)
- [ ] Design fits Bootstrap 5 system (floating labels, clean minimal)
- [ ] No more server-rendered login quirks
- [ ] Full TDD coverage
- [ ] Solves sessions_spec.rb test failure

## Dependencies
- Existing SessionsController JSON endpoints
- Bootstrap 5 UI components
- Pinia store infrastructure
- Vue Router setup

## Estimate
8-12 hours (TDD, design implementation, all providers, testing)

## References
- NuxtUI AuthForm pattern: https://ui.nuxt.com/components/auth-form
- Bootstrap 5 floating labels: https://getbootstrap.com/docs/5.3/forms/floating-labels/
- Rodauth migration planned after stabilization

## Notes
- Login2.vue exists but doesn't fit design system - use as reference only
- Follow API → Store → Composable → Page architecture
- Backend-agnostic design supports future Devise → Rodauth migration
- This permanently fixes the redirect loop test failures

# Phase 1: Backend API Test Plan

## Current State Analysis

### SessionsController#create (POST /users/sign_in)
✅ JSON support exists (lines 22-26)
- Returns: `{ user: { id, email, admin } }`
- Missing tests for JSON format

### SessionsController#destroy (DELETE /users/sign_out)  
❌ NO JSON support - only HTML redirects
- Needs: JSON format handler
- Returns: 204 No Content (standard for logout)

### Current User Endpoint
❌ Does NOT exist
- Needs: GET /api/current_user
- Should inherit from Api::BaseController
- Returns: `{ user: { id, email, admin } }` or 401

## Tests to Write (TDD - RED phase)

### 1. JSON Login Tests (sessions_spec.rb)
```ruby
describe 'POST /users/sign_in.json'
  - Success: valid credentials → 200 + user JSON
  - Error: invalid credentials → 401
  - Error: missing email → 400
  - Error: missing password → 400
```

### 2. JSON Logout Tests (sessions_spec.rb)
```ruby
describe 'DELETE /users/sign_out.json'
  - Success: authenticated user → 204 No Content
  - Success: clears session (verify with subsequent request)
  - No error for unauthenticated (logout is idempotent)
```

### 3. Current User Tests (spec/requests/api/current_user_spec.rb)
```ruby
describe 'GET /api/current_user'
  - Success: authenticated → 200 + user JSON
  - Error: unauthenticated → 401
```

## Implementation Plan (GREEN phase)

### Step 1: Add JSON to SessionsController#destroy
```ruby
def destroy
  respond_to do |format|
    format.json do
      sign_out(resource_name)
      head :no_content
    end
    format.html do
      # existing OIDC logic...
    end
  end
end
```

### Step 2: Create Api::CurrentUserController
```ruby
class Api::CurrentUserController < Api::BaseController
  def show
    if current_user
      render json: { user: current_user.as_json(only: %i[id email admin]) }
    else
      head :unauthorized
    end
  end
end
```

### Step 3: Add route
```ruby
# config/routes.rb
namespace :api do
  resource :current_user, only: [:show]
end
```

## Success Criteria
- [ ] All new tests written (RED)
- [ ] Tests fail as expected
- [ ] Implementation added (GREEN)
- [ ] All tests pass
- [ ] No existing tests broken

title: STANDARD: Code Quality Workflow
description: Code Quality Workflow Standard - Reference from hub card when committing code. Contains linting workflow, git commit safety protocol, production code rules, and common warning fixes.

comments.author: alippold
comments.text:
# Code Quality Workflow Standard

## Linting Workflow (Before Every Commit)

### 1. Run Linters
```bash
pnpm lint              # Auto-fixes + shows remaining warnings
bundle exec rubocop    # Backend linting
```

### 2. Fix Warnings Systematically
Priority order:
1. console.log statements (remove or convert to console.error/warn)
2. Missing emits declarations (add to defineEmits or emits array)
3. Unused variables (prefix with _ or remove)
4. ts/no-explicit-any (add proper types when working on that code)

### 3. Track Progress
- Use TodoWrite for multi-warning cleanup sessions
- Current baseline: 309 warnings (Jan 9, 2026)
- Goal: Trend down, not up

## Git Commit Safety Protocol

**CRITICAL - MANDATORY BEFORE ANY COMMIT:**

### Step 1: Review Changes
```bash
git status --short     # See what changed
git diff              # Review unstaged changes
git diff --staged     # Review staged changes (if any)
```

### Step 2: Stage Files Individually
```bash
# NEVER use:
git add -A            # ❌ FORBIDDEN
git add .             # ❌ FORBIDDEN
git add -u            # ❌ FORBIDDEN

# ALWAYS use:
git add path/to/specific/file.ts
git add path/to/another/file.vue
```

### Step 3: Verify Before Commit
```bash
git status --short    # Confirm ONLY intended files staged
git diff --staged     # Final review of what will be committed
```

### Step 4: Commit with Convention
```bash
git commit -m \"feat: Add user search to memberships

- Implemented Reka UI combobox with debounced search
- Added useMembers composable with search method
- Created searchUsers API endpoint
- Added 8 new tests (all passing)

Authored by: Aaron Lippold<lippold@gmail.com>\"
```

Commit Message Format:
- Prefix: feat:, fix:, test:, docs:, refactor:, chore:
- First line: Brief summary (50 chars max)
- Body: Bullet points of what changed
- Footer: Authored by: Aaron Lippold<lippold@gmail.com>
- NO Claude signatures

### Step 5: Sync
```bash
bd sync               # Sync beads changes
git push              # Push to remote
```

## Production Code Rules

NEVER commit:
- ❌ TODO comments (create beads card instead)
- ❌ console.log statements (use console.error/warn or remove)
- ❌ Failing tests
- ❌ Lint errors (warnings OK if tracking cleanup)
- ❌ Commented-out code (delete it)
- ❌ Unused variables without _ prefix

ALWAYS commit:
- ✅ Tests for new code
- ✅ Type definitions (no any for new code)
- ✅ Declared emits for Vue components
- ✅ Clean, working code

## Common Warning Fixes

### Unused variables:
```typescript
// ❌ Bad
const props = defineProps<{ item: IItem }>()

// ✅ Good (if actually unused)
const _props = defineProps<{ item: IItem }>()

// ✅ Better (remove if truly unused)
// (delete the line)
```

### Missing emits:
```vue
<!-- ❌ Bad -->
<script setup>
// No defineEmits
function handleClick() {
  emit('click')  // Warning!
}
</script>

<!-- ✅ Good -->
<script setup>
const emit = defineEmits<{ click: [] }>()
function handleClick() {
  emit('click')
}
</script>
```

### console.log:
```typescript
// ❌ Bad
console.log('Debug info:', data)

// ✅ Good (if legitimate logging)
console.error('Failed to load:', error)

// ✅ Better (remove debug statements)
// (delete the line)
```

title: STANDARD: Vue2→Vue3 Migration Pattern with TDD
description: # Vue2 → Vue3 Migration Standards **Reference this card when migrating any Vue component from Vue2 to Vue3** ## Architecture Pattern: API → Store → Composable → Page → Component ``` ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │ LAYER 1: API (apis/*.api.ts) │ │ - HTTP calls to Rails endpoints │ │ - Returns raw data, no state management │ │ - Example: searchUsers(projectId, query) │ ├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤ │ LAYER 2: STORE (stores/*.store.ts) │ │ - Pinia stores for state management │ │ - Caches data, handles loading/error states │ │ - Example: useMembersStore() with state + actions │ ├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤ │ LAYER 3: COMPOSABLE (composables/useXxx.ts) │ │ - Business logic, computed properties │ │ - Wraps store, provides reactive interface │ │ - Example: useMembers() exposes searchUsers, isLoading │ ├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤ │ LAYER 4: PAGE (pages/**/XxxPage.vue) │ │ - Uses composables via setup() │ │ - Minimal logic, delegates to composables │ │ - Example: ProjectShowPage.vue uses useMembers() │ ├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤ │ LAYER 5: COMPONENT (components/**/*.vue) │ │ - Reusable UI components │ │ - Props down, events up │ │ - Example: NewMembership.vue receives props, emits events │ └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ ``` ## Testing at Each Layer ### Layer 1: API Tests (vitest) ```typescript // apis/__tests__/members.api.spec.ts describe('searchUsers', () => { it('sends correct query params', async () => { mockAxios.get.mockResolvedValue({ data: { users: [] } }) await searchUsers(123, 'john') expect(mockAxios.get).toHaveBeenCalledWith('/api/projects/123/search_users', { params: { q: 'john' } }) }) }) ``` ### Layer 2: Store Tests (vitest) ```typescript // stores/__tests__/members.store.spec.ts describe('useMembersStore', () => { it('caches search results', async () => { const store = useMembersStore() await store.searchUsers(123, 'john') expect(store.searchResults).toHaveLength(3) }) }) ``` ### Layer 3: Composable Tests (vitest) ```typescript // composables/__tests__/useMembers.spec.ts describe('useMembers', () => { it('provides reactive search results', async () => { const { searchResults, searchUsers } = useMembers(123) await searchUsers('john') expect(searchResults.value).toHaveLength(3) }) }) ``` ### Layer 4: Component Tests (vitest + @vue/test-utils) ```typescript // components/__tests__/NewMembership.spec.ts describe('NewMembership', () => { it('displays search results dropdown', async () => { const wrapper = mount(NewMembership, { props: {...} }) const combobox = wrapper.find('[role="combobox"]') await combobox.setValue('john') expect(wrapper.find('[role="listbox"]').exists()).toBe(true) }) }) ``` ### Layer 5: Backend Tests (RSpec) ```ruby # spec/requests/api/projects_spec.rb RSpec.describe 'API::Projects', type: :request do describe 'GET /api/projects/:id/search_users' do it 'returns users matching query' do get "/api/projects/#{project.id}/search_users", params: { q: 'john' } expect(response).to have_http_status(:ok) expect(json['users']).to be_an(Array) end end end ``` ## TDD Workflow (MANDATORY) **ALWAYS follow this cycle for new features:** ``` 1. RED: Write failing test BEFORE implementation - Backend: RSpec test fails (feature doesn't exist) - Frontend: Vitest test fails (feature doesn't exist) 2. GREEN: Write minimal code to pass test - Implement ONLY what's needed to pass - No extra features, no refactoring yet 3. REFACTOR: Clean up while keeping tests green - Remove duplication - Improve names - Extract helpers - Tests must stay green 4. UPDATE TESTS: When migrating to new libraries - Component works, but tests need DOM structure updates - Example: Custom dropdown → Reka UI (selectors change) ``` **NEVER:** - Write code before tests - Commit code without passing tests - Leave TODO comments in production code - Skip test updates after library migrations ## Component Library Standards ### 1. Reka UI First (https://reka-ui.com/) **Default choice for new components** - headless UI primitives with full styling control Already installed: `reka-ui@2.6.0` **When to use Reka UI:** - Combobox (searchable dropdowns) - Dialog/Modal (command palette, modals) - Listbox (selectable lists) - Tabs, Accordion, Dropdown, etc. **Reka UI Patterns:** ```vue No results {{ item.name }} ``` **Reka UI Gotchas:** - ❌ Don't use `ComboboxPortal` in modals (breaks positioning) - ✅ Use inline `ComboboxContent` for modal contexts - ✅ Use `left: 0; right: 0;` for full width (not `width: 100%`) - ✅ Let Reka handle keyboard nav (don't implement manually) - ✅ `data-highlighted` is automatic, don't add custom `.highlighted` class ### 2. Bootstrap Vue Next (https://bootstrap-vue-next.github.io/) **For Bootstrap-specific components** Already installed: `bootstrap-vue-next@0.42.0` **When to use Bootstrap Vue Next:** - BTable (data tables) - BModal (modals - but consider Reka UI DialogRoot) - BButton, BAlert, BSpinner (basic UI) - BPagination, BFormInput, BFormSelect **Migration from Bootstrap Vue 2:** ```vue ``` **Changes:** - Component names: `b-table` → `BTable` (PascalCase) - Same props/events API (mostly compatible) - Some slots renamed (check docs) ### 3. Styling Priority 1. Bootstrap 5 utility classes (always first choice) 2. Bootstrap CSS variables for theming 3. Scoped component styles (minimal) ```vue ``` ## Vue 3 Migration Checklist ### Script Setup ```vue ``` ### Imports ```typescript // Always use TypeScript import type { IUser, IMembership } from '@/types' import { computed, ref, watch } from 'vue' import { useDebounceFn } from '@vueuse/core' ``` ### Reactivity ```typescript // ref() for primitives const count = ref(0) count.value = 10 // reactive() for objects (use sparingly) const state = reactive({ count: 0 }) state.count = 10 // computed() for derived values const doubled = computed(() => count.value * 2) ``` ## File Naming Conventions ``` apis/members.api.ts - API client functions stores/members.store.ts - Pinia store composables/useMembers.ts - Composable pages/projects/ShowPage.vue - Page component components/memberships/NewMembership.vue - Reusable component __tests__/members.api.spec.ts - API tests __tests__/members.store.spec.ts - Store tests __tests__/useMembers.spec.ts - Composable tests __tests__/NewMembership.spec.ts - Component tests ``` ## Common Migration Tasks ### 1. Async Search Dropdown → Reka UI Combobox - Replace custom dropdown HTML with Reka UI primitives - Remove manual keyboard navigation code - Remove manual scrollIntoView code - Keep debounced search with `useDebounceFn` - Update tests for Reka UI DOM structure ### 2. Bootstrap Vue 2 → Bootstrap Vue Next - Update component names (b-table → BTable) - Check slot changes (check docs per component) - Update imports (bootstrap-vue → bootstrap-vue-next) - Test all features (some behavior may differ) ### 3. Vuex → Pinia - Create Pinia store (stores/*.store.ts) - Use `defineStore()` with setup syntax - Replace mapState/mapGetters with composable - Replace mapActions with store methods ## Example: Complete Migration **Before (Vue 2):** ```vue ``` **After (Vue 3 + Architecture):** ```typescript // apis/users.api.ts export async function searchUsers(query: string) { const { data } = await http.get('/api/search', { params: { q: query } }) return data.users } // stores/users.store.ts export const useUsersStore = defineStore('users', () => { const searchResults = ref([]) async function search(query: string) { searchResults.value = await searchUsers(query) } return { searchResults, search } }) // composables/useUsers.ts export function useUsers() { const store = useUsersStore() const debouncedSearch = useDebounceFn(store.search, 300) return { searchResults: computed(() => store.searchResults), search: debouncedSearch } } ``` ```vue ``` ## Resources - Reka UI: https://reka-ui.com/ - Bootstrap Vue Next: https://bootstrap-vue-next.github.io/ - Vue 3 Docs: https://vuejs.org/ - Pinia Docs: https://pinia.vuejs.org/ - VueUse: https://vueuse.org/

title: PATTERN: Vue3 ShowPage Migration (benchmarks/stigs/srgs)
description:
Vue 3 ShowPage Migration Pattern - Reference this card at session start

## Working Examples
- pages/benchmarks/IndexPage.vue
- pages/stigs/ShowPage.vue  
- pages/srgs/ShowPage.vue

## Pattern

ShowPage: composable.fetchById() returns PLAIN OBJECT → pass as prop
DetailComponent: receives plain object prop, uses composable methods for updates

## Code Template

ShowPage.vue:
const { fetchById } = useItems()
const item = await fetchById(id)  // Returns item.value
<Detail :initial-state=item />

DetailComponent.vue:
props: { initialState: IItem }
const { update } = useItems()
await update(props.initialState.id, data)

## Rules
- fetchById returns PLAIN OBJECT not ref
- Pass plain object as prop
- Detail uses composable methods not direct API calls
- No http.get/axios in detail components

title: Fix HTML-only controller responses for SPA consistency
description:
## Problem

Four controllers have HTML-only responses causing page reloads in Vue SPA:

1. **UsersController#destroy** (app/controllers/users_controller.rb:54-61)
   - Vue: UsersTable.vue uses submitDelete (HTML form)
   - Controller: HTML_ONLY (flash + redirect_to action: 'index')
   
2. **MembershipsController#destroy** (app/controllers/memberships_controller.rb:80-94)
   - Vue: MembershipsTable.vue uses submitDelete (HTML form)
   - Controller: HTML_ONLY (flash + redirect_to @membership.membership)
   
3. **ProjectsController#destroy** (app/controllers/projects_controller.rb:127-135)
   - Vue: ProjectsTable.vue uses axios.delete (already correct\!)
   - Controller: HTML_ONLY (flash + redirect_to action: 'index')
   
4. **MembershipsController#create** (app/controllers/memberships_controller.rb:10-42)
   - Used by NewMembership.vue via HTML form submission
   - Controller: HTML_ONLY (flash + redirect_to membership.membership)

## Impact

- Breaks SPA experience with full page reloads
- Inconsistent with other controllers (ComponentsController, RulesController all JSON_ONLY)
- CSRF tokens work (fixed globally in Session 110)

## Solution

Apply Session 110 pattern to each:

**Frontend (Vue components):**
1. Replace `submitDelete` with `axios.delete`
2. Add toast notifications (success/error)
3. Handle response and update UI

**Backend (Rails controllers):**
1. Add `respond_to do |format|` blocks
2. Support both HTML and JSON formats
3. Return proper status codes and messages

**Testing:**
1. Add request specs for JSON responses
2. Test success and error cases

## Files to Modify

**Vue Components:**
- app/javascript/components/users/UsersTable.vue
- app/javascript/components/memberships/MembershipsTable.vue
- app/javascript/components/memberships/NewMembership.vue (if create is used)

**Controllers:**
- app/controllers/users_controller.rb (destroy)
- app/controllers/memberships_controller.rb (create, destroy)
- app/controllers/projects_controller.rb (destroy)

**Tests:**
- spec/requests/users_spec.rb (add JSON tests)
- spec/requests/memberships_spec.rb (add JSON tests)
- spec/requests/projects_spec.rb (add JSON tests)

## Reference

See Session 110 fixes:
- commit cca76ff: OIDC login and access request workflows
- MembershipsTable.vue rejectRequest() - axios pattern
- ProjectAccessRequestsController - respond_to pattern

title: Requirements Editor Phase 2: Focus View Refactor
Audit docs for STIG terminology precision + non-STIG scope
description:
## Background

Two related concerns surfaced during PR #731 doc work that warrant a focused audit of the existing VitePress doc set:

### 1. STIG terminology precision (legally important)

A document is not a 'STIG' until DISA formally publishes it. Calling pre-publication work in Vulcan a 'STIG' misrepresents formal DoD policy status — the same trap that bites vendors who claim 'we wrote a STIG' (or worse, 'we can help you write your own STIG') when they have not actually engaged DISA.

The SAF training (saf.mitre.org) deliberately uses 'STIG-ready security guidance documentation' or 'security guidance document' for in-progress / pre-publication work, reserving 'STIG' only for published DISA artifacts. Vulcan's docs should match that discipline.

### 2. Non-STIG scope

Vulcan is primarily used for STIG-ready authoring today, but is intended to broaden into a general security-guidance authoring tool. Docs that lock the tool to STIGs only (in language, examples, or framing) create a future migration cost and limit current adoption by non-STIG teams.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Audit every page in docs/ for the word 'STIG' and similar phrasing ('write a STIG', 'STIG creation', 'STIG content', etc.).
- [ ] For each occurrence, decide: is this referencing a published DISA STIG (keep), or is it referring to in-progress Vulcan content (rewrite)?
- [ ] In-progress content gets renamed to 'security guidance document', 'STIG-ready security guidance documentation', 'draft requirements', or similar precise terminology per SAF training conventions.
- [ ] Where the docs frame Vulcan as a STIG-only tool, broaden to 'security guidance authoring' / 'security requirements authoring' framing.
- [ ] Spot-check the DISA Process section — those pages are explicitly about the DISA workflow and can keep STIG terminology where accurate.
- [ ] Cross-reference SAF training site language for tone.

## Out of scope

- The DISA Process section (disa-process/*) is explicitly about engaging DISA's STIG publication process; STIG terminology is correct there.
- Code-side terminology (model names, identifiers like comment_phase, etc.) — those are internal and don't need this pass.
- Marketing copy outside the docs.

## Notes

This was surfaced while writing the new Comment Triage primer (Public Comment Review page). The primer itself was caught and corrected in the same review, but the rest of the doc set hasn't been audited. Likely candidates needing attention: overview.md, authoring-rules.md, getting-started/quick-start.md, the home page, release notes.

title: Dark mode: inconsistent gray backgrounds across comment + decision UI
description:
Reported during v2-05f.46 verification (2026-06-01). In dark mode several components render with a different shade of gray background that does not match the surrounding container or the underlying dark theme token:

- The active-comment card in the split-pane triage view (the section that shows the commenter name + comment body + reaction buttons) shows a lighter-gray box instead of inheriting the container background.
- The Decision picker block (Decision radios + section labels) shows the same off-color gray.
- A reply CommentThread sitting under a parent whose triage status is 'concur_with_comment' (blue tint applied per v2-05f.46) still has a gray container around the reply collapsible — the inner reply card body picks up the parent tint correctly, but the wrapping container does not. Distinct from the v2-05f.46 fix which addressed the reply CARDS specifically.
- In light mode the same areas do not show an equivalent gradient; the inconsistency is dark-mode-specific.

Suspect: hardcoded background-color values or non-themed CSS vars in CommentTriageForm, the active-comment card region of TriageSplitView, and the CommentThread root wrapper. Should audit these for --vulcan-component-bg / --vulcan-body-bg adherence and remove any literal grays.

AC:
- [ ] Active-comment region in TriageSplitView inherits the dark theme container bg
- [ ] CommentTriageForm Decision block inherits the dark theme container bg
- [ ] CommentThread wrapper does not introduce its own gray box (matches parent row tint)
- [ ] Light mode unchanged (no regressions)
- [ ] Audit all .triage-* and .comment-* classes for literal background-color values
close_reason: Closed — combination of Aaron's dark-mode work (b7cd5be5 dark mode foundation: three-tier bg hierarchy + active state + table header contrast; 8fb96604 triage split-pane dark mode: three-tier bg + panel borders; 26396e16 split-pane shared panel pattern: padding + bg + borders) and my CommentThread .thread-replies wrapper tint (commit 0ff19347) cover all three originally-reported gray-box areas: active-comment region (Aaron's --content panel), Decision picker (Aaron's --form panel), reply collapsible wrapper (my parentTriageBgClass on .thread-replies). Re-verify in-app; reopen if any single area still drifts off-theme.

title: Backfill + default NULL triage_status on imported top-level comments
description:
Imported top-level comments can land with triage_status=NULL: Review#default_triage_status_for_new_top_level_comment is a before_create callback, but JSON-archive import uses bulk Review.insert! (bypasses callbacks), and ReviewBuilder#lifecycle_attrs only copies triage_status when the source archive has it present. A top-level comment untriaged in the source instance therefore imports as NULL. Effects: progress bar underfills (track bg shows ~1/total), the comment is unreachable via the status filter (where triage_status='pending' excludes NULL), and resolvedCount (total - pending) overcounts. Confirmed on component 30: status group = {concur 1, concur_with_comment 19, duplicate 4, informational 1, pending 87, nil 1}, total 113.

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] Migration backfills triage_status='pending' for top-level comments (action='comment', responding_to_review_id IS NULL) where triage_status IS NULL; replies (responding_to_review_id present) keep NULL
- [ ] ReviewBuilder defaults top-level comment triage_status to 'pending' when the archive value is blank, so re-imports do not reintroduce NULL
- [ ] Reply imports keep NULL triage_status (replies_cannot_have_triage_status still holds)
- [ ] After fix, CommentQueryService status_counts named buckets sum to total (no nil bucket for top-level)
- [ ] TDD: failing test first; no regressions

title: Replies inherit parent comment triage-status background when adjudicated

title: Research and ship API docs viewer — Scalar vs openapi-explorer
description:
Title: Research and ship API docs viewer — Scalar vs openapi-explorer

Description:
Add a browsable API docs page backed by doc/openapi.yaml. Compare two zero-build options (Scalar via CDN, openapi-explorer web component) and ship the one that best fits Vue 2.7 + Bootstrap-Vue + Turbolinks. The spec already exists; this card adds the consumer-facing UI.
Design doc: N/A — research goes in card notes

Files:
- Create: app/views/api_docs/show.html.haml (viewer mount point)
- Create: app/controllers/api_docs_controller.rb (single show action, admin-gated)
- Modify: config/routes.rb (GET /api-docs route)
- Modify: app/views/layouts/application.html.haml or navbar (link if appropriate)
- Test: spec/requests/api_docs_spec.rb (route renders, auth gate)
- Test: spec/system/api_docs_spec.rb (page loads, spec parses)

First failing test:
spec/requests/api_docs_spec.rb — 'GET /api-docs requires admin and renders viewer'

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] Comparison table in card notes covers: bundle size, Vue 2.7 compat, Turbolinks behavior, dark mode, search/try-it-out, accessibility, maintenance status, license
- [ ] Decision recorded in card notes with rationale before implementation
- [ ] Single route GET /api-docs renders the chosen viewer against /doc/openapi.yaml
- [ ] Admin-only by default (authorize_admin)
- [ ] Works under Turbolinks (mount/unmount on turbolinks:load)

- [ ] All work via TDD (failing test first)
- [ ] No regressions on existing tests

Verification:
bundle exec rspec spec/requests/api_docs_spec.rb spec/system/api_docs_spec.rb && yarn lint

Decision points:
- Scalar CDN vs npm install (CDN simpler, npm pins version + works offline)
- openapi-explorer Web Component vs Scalar Vue component (Vue 2.7 has Composition API backport, so either should work)
- Admin-only vs viewer+ access (start admin; widen later if useful)
- Whether to bundle the viewer in an existing pack or create a new api_docs pack

Anti-patterns:
- Do NOT pick a viewer without reading both READMEs and the comparison criteria
- Do NOT serve the spec from a third-party CDN — own the spec URL
- Do NOT skip Turbolinks lifecycle hooks (caused breakage in DiffViewer historically)
- Do NOT add npm dependencies without checking bundle size impact

NOT in scope:
- Extending the OpenAPI spec itself (other cards)
- Authoring new endpoints
- Public/unauthenticated access (separate card if wanted)
- A multi-spec selector (single spec for now)

notes: [2026-06-01] Released back to OPEN — ACs require real implementation (controller + view + admin gate + Turbolinks + system spec), not just research. Sized too big for a quick-win throughput pass; needs a focused session. Comparison sketch: Scalar CDN is simplest (one <script>+web-component tag) but pins version; openapi-explorer is similar shape. Both Vue-agnostic web components. Either works under Turbolinks if we mount on turbolinks:load. Recommend Scalar via npm (version-pinned, offline-able) for a future session.

title: Audit and reduce comment density across the repo
description:
Comments have accumulated across the codebase that don't earn their keep — restated code, card/bead-name tags in app files ("# vulcan-v3.x-XYZ: …"), and multi-line WHY blocks on routine fixes. Drive comment density down by deleting comments that don't help a future reader.

Apply the project's commenting rule: default to NO comment; ≤1 line only when a reader would otherwise be confused; multi-line WHY only for hidden constraints the code can't express (concurrent-write race, audit-trail invariant, browser quirk). Card/bead references belong in commit messages, NOT in code.

Categories to target:
- Card/bead-name comments in app/controllers, app/models, app/javascript, db/migrate, config/routes.rb (search for 'vulcan-v3.x-' or '(card-id)' patterns in code)
- Restated-code comments ('# loop through users', '# spread before sort')
- Multi-line WHY blocks on simple bug fixes / single-line changes
- Per-section / per-method banner comments that add no information

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] Sweep results in net comment-line reduction across app/, db/migrate/, config/, doc/
- [ ] No bead/card-name strings remain in code comments
- [ ] Comments that DO survive earn their keep: explain a hidden constraint, point at an upstream bug/issue, or document a non-obvious tradeoff
- [ ] Tests still pass (parallel_rspec spec/ + yarn test:unit)
- [ ] RuboCop + ESLint clean

Verification:
bundle exec parallel_rspec spec/ && yarn test:unit
grep -rn 'vulcan-v3\.x-' app/ db/migrate/ config/ doc/ || echo 'clean'

NOT in scope:
- Documentation in docs/ (separate effort if needed)
- AGENTS.md / CLAUDE.md files
- Changing code behavior — comment-only edits

title: Replace CommentQueryService .ids materialization with subqueries — reduce memory allocation
description:
Title: Replace CommentQueryService .ids materialization with subqueries — reduce memory allocation

Description:
CommentQueryService#serialize_rows (line 118) calls @component.rules.ids which materializes all rule IDs into a Ruby array, then passes to RuleSatisfaction.where(rule_id: ids). For a 300-rule component, this is 300 integers loaded into Ruby memory and sent back to PostgreSQL as an IN(...) list. Replace with @component.rules.select(:id) subquery — PostgreSQL handles it server-side without round-tripping IDs through Ruby.
Design doc: N/A — from v2.3.1+ performance regression analysis

Files:
- Modify: app/services/comment_query_service.rb
- Test: spec/services/comment_query_service_spec.rb

First failing test:
spec/services/comment_query_service_spec.rb — 'serialize_rows uses subquery for rule_satisfactions lookup'

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] @component.rules.ids replaced with @component.rules.select(:id) subquery
- [ ] RuleSatisfaction and Review child-count queries use subquery instead of IN(...) array
- [ ] Response data unchanged
- [ ] Eliminates 1 materialization query
- [ ] All work via TDD (failing test first)
- [ ] No regressions on existing tests

Verification:
bundle exec rspec spec/services/comment_query_service_spec.rb && bundle exec parallel_rspec spec/

Decision points:
- None expected

Anti-patterns:
- Do NOT use .pluck(:id) — that also materializes; use .select(:id) for subquery
- Do NOT change the response shape

NOT in scope:
- Caching rule IDs across requests
- Changing the pagination logic

Before closing:
- [ ] Re-read each AC checkbox — verify with evidence
- [ ] Re-read Anti-patterns — confirm none violated
- [ ] Run the exact Verification command — paste output
- [ ] git diff shows ONLY files listed in Files section

Estimate: 8 min
close_reason: Done. Estimated ~8 min, actual ~5 min. Replaced @component.rules.ids with rule_id_subquery in RuleSatisfaction lookup. Eliminates materialization of 300+ rule IDs into Ruby array — PostgreSQL handles it server-side via IN(SELECT ...). 13 CQS specs pass. RuboCop clean.

title: Add error path tests to all 9 API test files — rejected promises, network errors
description:
Title: Add error path tests to all 9 API test files — rejected promises, network errors

Description:
All 71 existing API tests only cover the success path. Zero tests use mockRejectedValue(). This means the API layer's error propagation is completely unverified — if a function swallows an error or transforms it incorrectly, no test catches it. Add error path tests for at least one function per module (9 tests minimum) plus edge case tests for null/empty params.
Design doc: N/A

title: Fix table responsiveness — mobile-first layout for all b-table pages
description:
Title: Fix table responsiveness — mobile-first layout for all b-table pages

Description:
All b-table pages (Projects, STIGs, SRGs, Released Components, Users, Triage) render poorly on smaller viewports. Columns overflow, text truncates without indication, and horizontal scrolling is required. Bootstrap-Vue's b-table has built-in stacked mode (stacked="md") but it's not used consistently. Need to research mobile-first table best practices (Bootstrap-Vue stacked, responsive wrapper, column priority/hiding) and apply a consistent pattern across all table pages.

Research needed:
- Bootstrap-Vue b-table stacked="md" vs responsive wrapper
- Bootstrap 5 responsive table patterns
- Tailwind/Nuxt UI table responsive patterns
- Column priority: which columns to show/hide at breakpoints
- Whether to use horizontal scroll, stacked cards, or column hiding

Files:
- Modify: app/javascript/components/security_requirements_guides/SecurityRequirementsGuidesTable.vue
- Modify: app/javascript/components/projects/ProjectsTable.vue (or equivalent)
- Modify: app/javascript/components/users/UsersTable.vue (or equivalent)
- Modify: app/javascript/components/triage/CommentTable.vue (or equivalent)
- Test: Playwright viewport resize verification at 768px, 576px, 375px

First failing test:
Playwright: navigate to /projects at 576px viewport width — table should not require horizontal scroll

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] Research completed: documented approach in card notes before implementing
- [ ] All b-table instances use consistent responsive pattern (stacked or responsive wrapper)
- [ ] Projects table readable at 768px (tablet) and 576px (phone)
- [ ] STIGs/SRGs table readable at 768px and 576px
- [ ] Triage comment table readable at 768px
- [ ] Low-priority columns hidden at small breakpoints (e.g., Description, Last Updated)
- [ ] No horizontal overflow at any standard breakpoint

- [ ] Dark mode appearance maintained at all breakpoints
- [ ] All work via TDD (failing test first)
- [ ] No regressions on existing tests

Verification:
Playwright viewport resize to 768px + 576px on /projects, /stigs, /srgs — no horizontal scroll, content readable

Decision points:
- Whether to use stacked="md" (cards layout) or responsive (scroll wrapper) or column hiding
- Which columns to hide at each breakpoint — consult with user
- Whether to add a column visibility toggle for users to customize

Anti-patterns:
- Do NOT just add overflow-x: auto and call it done — that's a band-aid
- Do NOT hide essential columns without user feedback on priority
- Do NOT apply different patterns to different tables — ONE consistent approach

NOT in scope:
- Component editor sidebar responsiveness (separate card vulcan-v3.x-3le)

- Modal responsiveness

title: Fix sidebar + text-muted + link colors — component surface differentiation
description:
Title: Fix sidebar + surfaces + Shiki theme + text-muted — editor dark mode integration

Description:
Three connected issues on the component editor in dark mode: (1) Sidebar has no bg differentiation from main content. (2) Shiki syntax highlighter always renders with github-light theme — the github-dark theme is loaded but never used. highlightCode() defaults to "github-light" and the fallback hardcodes style="background-color:#fff". (3) MarkdownTextarea.vue has hardcoded .shiki { background-color: #f6f8fa !important } that overrides dark mode. (4) Editor page surface hierarchy is inconsistent — filter panels, toolbar, sidebar, and content area all blend together.

Research:
- Shiki supports dual themes: createHighlighterCoreSync already loads github-light + github-dark
- highlightCode(code, lang, { theme: 'github-dark' }) works — just needs data-bs-theme detection
- Nuxt UI v4 bg hierarchy: bg (900) → bg-muted (800) → bg-elevated (800) → bg-accented (700)
- Material Design M2 surface elevation: body (darkest) → cards/panels (+5-7% white) → elevated (+12%)

Connected files:
- app/javascript/utilities/syntaxHighlighter.js — highlightCode() always uses github-light
- app/javascript/components/shared/MarkdownTextarea.vue — hardcoded .shiki bg + previewRender uses highlightCode
- app/javascript/application.scss — surface overrides needed

Files:
- Modify: app/javascript/utilities/syntaxHighlighter.js (detect data-bs-theme, use github-dark)
- Modify: app/javascript/components/shared/MarkdownTextarea.vue (dark mode .shiki CSS override)
- Modify: app/javascript/application.scss (.left-sidebar-column bg, filter panel surface hierarchy)
- Test: spec/config/dark_mode_compiled_spec.rb (sidebar selector test)
- Test: spec/javascript/utils/syntaxHighlighter.spec.js (theme detection test if exists)

Acceptance criteria:

- [ ] Filter panels (Status/Display/Review) use component-bg to show elevation
- [ ] Editor page surface hierarchy is consistent: body(900) → panels/sidebar(800) → inputs(800 distinct border)

title: Fix badge + alert dark mode colors — desaturated per Material Design
description:
Title: Fix badge + alert dark mode colors — desaturated per Material Design

Description:
Bootstrap 4 badges (.badge-warning, .badge-info, .badge-success, etc.) use fully saturated light-mode background colors that are harsh on dark surfaces. Material Design M2 specifically warns against saturated colors on dark: "avoid using saturated colors — they produce optical vibrations against a dark background causing eye strain." Bootstrap 5.3 provides bg-subtle variants (shade-color 80%) and text-emphasis variants (tint-color 40%) for this exact purpose.

Research:
- Material Design M2: "dark theme should avoid using saturated colors" — use 200 tonal value
- Bootstrap 5.3: .badge uses --bs-badge-color and --bs-badge-bg CSS vars. Alert variants use shade-color 80% for bg, tint-color 40% for text in dark mode.
- Nuxt UI v4: semantic colors shift 500→400 (one stop lighter, slightly desaturated)
- Color theory: fully saturated yellow (#ffc107) on dark gray (#212529) causes halation — the bright color bleeds at edges

Alert dark mode already partially handled (fad.2 added alert overrides with rgba). This card focuses on BADGES specifically.

Files:
- Modify: app/javascript/application.scss (.badge-* overrides in [data-bs-theme="dark"])
- Test: spec/config/dark_mode_compiled_spec.rb (assertions for badge selectors)

First failing test:
spec/config/dark_mode_compiled_spec.rb — 'has dark override for .badge-warning' — asserts [data-bs-theme="dark"] .badge-warning has background-color declaration that is not the raw #ffc107

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] .badge-warning bg adjusted — use mix(white, $warning, 20%) bg with dark text, or rgba($warning, 0.2) bg with tinted text
- [ ] .badge-info bg adjusted for dark mode
- [ ] .badge-success bg adjusted for dark mode
- [ ] .badge-danger bg adjusted for dark mode
- [ ] .badge-secondary bg adjusted (already dim — may need lightening not darkening)
- [ ] Playwright: badge-warning computed bg ≠ rgb(255, 193, 7) on /projects table
- [ ] Playwright: all badge text passes WCAG AA 4.5:1 against badge bg
- [ ] Playwright: visually verify "18 pending" badges on /projects are readable without eye strain
- [ ] Light mode badges unchanged
- [ ] All work via TDD (failing test first)
- [ ] No regressions on existing tests

Verification:
yarn build && bundle exec rspec spec/config/dark_mode_compiled_spec.rb && Playwright verify badges on /projects + /components/29/triage

Decision points:
- Whether to use opaque desaturated bg (Material pattern) or translucent bg with tinted text (BS5.3 subtle pattern)
- Whether .badge-primary needs adjustment (blue on dark is usually fine)

Anti-patterns:
- Do NOT use fully saturated colors on dark backgrounds (Material Design rule)
- Do NOT change badge text to white-on-saturated — that's the light mode pattern
- Do NOT hardcode hex — use Sass mix() or rgba()

NOT in scope:
- Triage status badges (those use --triage-* CSS vars from the design system — separate layer)
- Alert adjustments (already done in fad.2)
- CAT severity badges (separate card if needed)

Before closing:
- [ ] Re-read each AC checkbox — verify with evidence
- [ ] Re-read Anti-patterns — confirm none violated
- [ ] Run the exact Verification command — paste output
- [ ] git diff shows ONLY files listed in Files section

Story points: sp:3
Estimate: 15 min
close_reason: Done. Estimated ~15 min, actual ~6 min. All 5 badge variants desaturated with mix(black, $color, 60%) bg + tinted 40% text per Material Design + BS5.3. Playwright verified on: /projects — warning/info badges confirmed desaturated. Light mode regression passed.

title: Fix navbar link opacity + table header bg — legibility baseline
description:
Title: Fix navbar link opacity + table header bg — legibility baseline

Description:
Two legibility issues affecting every page: (1) Navbar .nav-link color is rgba(255,255,255,0.5) from Bootstrap 4's .navbar-dark — too dim at 50% opacity. Bootstrap 5.3 uses rgba(255,255,255,0.75) (55% increase). (2) Table thead th has no background differentiation from body rows in dark mode. Bootstrap 5.3 uses --bs-table-bg for header differentiation. Tailwind uses dark:bg-gray-800 for elevated surfaces.

Research:
- Bootstrap 5.3 _navbar.scss: --bs-navbar-active-color: rgba(255,255,255,0.9), --bs-nav-link-color: rgba(255,255,255,0.75)

title: Fix outline button colors — tint-color 40% per Bootstrap 5.3
description:
Title: Fix outline button colors — tint-color 40% per Bootstrap 5.3

Description:
Bootstrap 4's .btn-outline-secondary uses text/border color #6c757d ($secondary) which has ~2.5:1 contrast on dark surfaces — fails WCAG AA. Bootstrap 5.3 solves this with tint-color($color, 40%) for text-emphasis dark variants. We need to override every .btn-outline-* variant under [data-bs-theme="dark"] using mix(white, $color, 40%) (BS4 equivalent of tint-color).

Research:
- Bootstrap 5.3 _variables-dark.scss: $secondary-text-emphasis-dark = tint-color($secondary, 40%) → #a7acb1
- Nuxt UI v4: semantic colors shift from 500→400 in dark mode (same direction — lighter)
- Material Design M2: use 200 tonal value for primary on dark surfaces (lighter, desaturated)
- WCAG AA: 4.5:1 minimum contrast for body text

Computed values (mix(white, $color, 40%)):
- secondary: #6c757d → #a7acb1
- primary: #007bff → #66a9ff
- success: #28a745 → #6dc486
- danger: #dc3545 → #e97a84
- warning: #ffc107 → #ffd45a
- info: #17a2b8 → #5bbfcf

Files:
- Modify: app/javascript/application.scss (add .btn-outline-* overrides in [data-bs-theme="dark"] block)
- Test: spec/config/dark_mode_compiled_spec.rb (new assertions for outline button selectors)

First failing test:
spec/config/dark_mode_compiled_spec.rb — 'has dark override for .btn-outline-secondary' — asserts [data-bs-theme="dark"] .btn-outline-secondary has color and border-color declarations

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] .btn-outline-secondary text/border uses mix(white, $secondary, 40%) ≈ #a7acb1 in dark mode
- [ ] .btn-outline-primary text/border uses mix(white, $primary, 40%) in dark mode
- [ ] .btn-outline-success text/border uses mix(white, $success, 40%) in dark mode
- [ ] .btn-outline-danger text/border uses mix(white, $danger, 40%) in dark mode
- [ ] .btn-outline-warning text/border uses mix(white, $warning, 40%) in dark mode
- [ ] .btn-outline-info text/border uses mix(white, $info, 40%) in dark mode
- [ ] Playwright: computed color on .btn-outline-secondary ≠ rgb(108, 117, 125) in dark mode
- [ ] Playwright: visually verify on /projects/6 (has outline buttons for Members, Triage, Download, Details, etc.)
- [ ] Light mode outline buttons unchanged
- [ ] All work via TDD (failing test first)
- [ ] No regressions on existing tests

Verification:
yarn build && bundle exec rspec spec/config/dark_mode_compiled_spec.rb && Playwright computed-style check on /projects/6 .btn-outline-secondary

Decision points:
- Whether to adjust solid .btn-primary etc. (Bootstrap 5.3 does NOT — verify they look fine as-is in Playwright)
- Whether .btn-outline-warning hover state needs separate override

Anti-patterns:
- Do NOT hardcode hex values — use mix(white, $color, 40%) Sass formula
- Do NOT guess what the colors look like — verify in Playwright
- Do NOT change solid button colors unless they fail contrast check

NOT in scope:
- Solid button adjustments (btn-primary, btn-danger — stay unchanged per BS5.3)
- Hover/focus/active state adjustments (card those separately if needed after Playwright review)
- Button sizing changes

Before closing:

- [ ] Re-read Anti-patterns — confirm none violated

- [ ] git diff shows ONLY files listed in Files section

title: Fix body + foundation dark mode — body bg/color override
description:
Title: Fix body + foundation dark mode — body bg/color override

Description:
Bootstrap 4 compiles body { background-color: #fff; color: #212529 } as hardcoded values. The [data-bs-theme="dark"] block sets these on html, but body paints over html. Bootstrap 5.3 solves this by using CSS variables in body (body { background-color: var(--bs-body-bg) }). Since BS4 can't do that, we explicitly override body under [data-bs-theme="dark"].

Research: Bootstrap 5.3 _reboot.scss body rule uses var(--bs-body-bg). Material Design M2 specifies #121212 as base dark surface. We use Bootstrap's $gray-900 (#212529) which is the same value BS5.3 uses for --bs-body-bg-dark.

Files:
- Modify: app/javascript/application.scss (add body rule inside [data-bs-theme="dark"])
- Test: spec/config/dark_mode_compiled_spec.rb (new assertions for body selector)

First failing test:
spec/config/dark_mode_compiled_spec.rb — 'overrides body background-color in dark mode' — asserts [data-bs-theme="dark"] body { background-color } exists in compiled CSS

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] [data-bs-theme="dark"] body has background-color override (not just html)
- [ ] [data-bs-theme="dark"] body has color override
- [ ] Playwright foundation check passes: body bg = rgb(33, 37, 41), body color = rgb(222, 226, 230)
- [ ] Foundation check passes on at least 3 different pages (/projects, /components/:id, /components/:id/triage)
- [ ] Light mode body bg still white (regression check)
- [ ] All work via TDD (failing test first)
- [ ] No regressions on existing tests

Verification:
yarn build && bundle exec rspec spec/config/dark_mode_compiled_spec.rb && Playwright evaluate foundation check on /projects

Decision points:
- None — straightforward fix following Bootstrap 5.3 pattern

Anti-patterns:
- Do NOT set bg on html element only — body has its own bg that overrides
- Do NOT close without Playwright foundation check on 3+ pages

NOT in scope:
- Component-level overrides (separate cards)
- Color value adjustments (separate card)

- [ ] Re-read each AC checkbox — verify with evidence
- [ ] Re-read Anti-patterns — confirm none violated
- [ ] Run the exact Verification command — paste output
- [ ] git diff shows ONLY files listed in Files section

Estimate: 5 min (already partially done — test written, CSS added, needs Playwright verify)
close_reason: Done. Estimated ~5 min, actual ~3 min (test+fix done earlier, verification this pass). Body bg/color override under [data-bs-theme=dark] body {}. Playwright verified on: /projects, /components/29, /components/29/triage. Light mode regression check passed.

title: [EPIC] Fix dark mode color correctness — research-backed color adjustments
description:
Title: [EPIC] Fix dark mode color correctness — research-backed color adjustments

Description:
Dark mode was implemented with raw light-mode Bootstrap 4 color values that produce poor contrast, harsh saturation, and invisible elements on dark surfaces. This sub-epic applies research-backed color corrections from Bootstrap 5.3, Nuxt UI v4, Tailwind CSS, and Google Material Design M2/M3. Each card targets one category of color issue with TDD + Playwright verification.

Research references:
- Bootstrap 5.3: tint-color($color, 40%) for text-emphasis, shade-color($color, 80%) for bg-subtle
- Nuxt UI v4: semantic colors shift 500→400 in dark mode, text 700→200, bg white→neutral-900
- Tailwind: gray text shifts ~1-2 stops lighter (gray-500→gray-400)
- Material Design M2: use 200 tonal value (not 500-700), desaturate to prevent optical vibrations
- Material Design M2: surface elevation via white overlay (1dp=5%, 2dp=7%, 8dp=12%, 16dp=15%)
- Material Design M2: text opacity hierarchy — high=87%, medium=60%, disabled=38%
- WCAG AA: 4.5:1 minimum contrast for body text at all elevation surfaces

Core formula for Vulcan (Bootstrap 4 adaptation):
  mix(white, $color, 40%) = Bootstrap 5.3's tint-color($color, 40%)
  This is the universal dark-mode text/outline/emphasis color adjustment.

Files:
- See child cards

First failing test:
See child cards

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] All outline buttons readable on dark surfaces (WCAG AA 4.5:1)
- [ ] Navbar links legible (opacity raised from 0.5 to 0.75+)
- [ ] Table headers visually differentiated from body rows
- [ ] Badge colors appropriate brightness for dark surfaces
- [ ] Text-muted readable on dark bg (gray-400 not gray-600)
- [ ] Sidebar visually differentiated from main content
- [ ] Every page Playwright-verified in dark mode (foundation check passes)
- [ ] Light mode completely unchanged (regression check)
- [ ] All work via TDD (failing test first)
- [ ] No regressions on existing tests
- [ ] /dark-mode-verify skill executed on every change

Verification:
yarn build && bundle exec rspec spec/config/dark_mode_compiled_spec.rb && Playwright visual review of all 9 page types in both modes

Decision points:
- Whether solid buttons (btn-primary, btn-danger) need any adjustment (BS5.3 says no — verify)
- Whether to adjust badge bg-color or just text-color for dark mode

Anti-patterns:
- Do NOT write CSS without verifying selectors match real DOM elements (querySelector first)
- Do NOT close cards without Playwright computed-style + screenshot verification
- Do NOT use saturated/vibrant colors on dark surfaces (causes optical vibrations per Material Design)
- Do NOT change light mode appearance
- Do NOT guess color values — use mix(white, $color, 40%) formula from BS5.3 research

NOT in scope:
- Bootstrap 5 migration
- Vue 3 migration
- Color palette redesign or rebranding
- Per-user theme preference in database
- Login/auth page dark mode (separate pack)
- Animation or transition effects for mode switching

Before closing:

- [ ] Re-read Anti-patterns — confirm none violated
- [ ] Run the exact Verification command — paste output
- [ ] All child cards closed with Playwright evidence

Estimate: 90 min Claude-pace
close_reason: Done. All 7 child cards closed. Dark mode color corrections complete with 9-page Playwright audit. Research-backed (BS5.3, Material Design, Nuxt UI, Tailwind). 40 compiled CSS tests. SeverityBadges shared component. Body fix, outline buttons, badges, tooltips, vue-multiselect, Shiki, all verified.

title: Dark mode — logo handling, shadows, border sweep, final polish
description:

NOT in scope:

title: [EPIC] Vulcan Dark Mode — Bootstrap 5.3 data-bs-theme pattern on Bootstrap 4
description:
Title: [EPIC] Vulcan Dark Mode — Bootstrap 5.3 data-bs-theme pattern on Bootstrap 4

Description:
Add dark mode to Vulcan v2.x using Bootstrap 5.3's data-bs-theme attribute pattern adapted for Bootstrap 4.6.2. Foundation already built (Layer 1 --vulcan-* CSS custom properties bridge). Dark mode = override those variables under [data-bs-theme="dark"]. OS preference detection via @media (prefers-color-scheme: dark) with user override via toggle.

Audit findings: 59 Vue/HAML files using Bootstrap components, 128 white backgrounds, 34 bg-light utilities, ~777 light border instances. EasyMDE and Monaco editors need custom dark CSS. Estimated ~850-1000 lines of dark mode CSS.

Files:
- See child cards

First failing test:
See child cards

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] data-bs-theme="dark" attribute on html element switches all colors
- [ ] Body, card, modal, dropdown, form, table backgrounds inverted
- [ ] Border colors inverted (gray-300 → gray-700 pattern)
- [ ] Text colors inverted (gray-900 → gray-300 pattern)
- [ ] Semantic colors adjusted (tint-color for dark bg readability)
- [ ] --vulcan-* variables overridden under [data-bs-theme="dark"]
- [ ] --triage-* colors auto-adjust via the var() chain
- [ ] EasyMDE markdown editor dark theme
- [ ] Monaco code editor dark theme
- [ ] Toggle button in navbar (persists to localStorage)
- [ ] OS prefers-color-scheme fallback (no JS needed)
- [ ] Subtree scoping works (dark sidebar + light content)
- [ ] Classification banner colors unaffected (DoD standard)
- [ ] Zero visual regressions in light mode
- [ ] All work via TDD
- [ ] No regressions

Verification:
yarn test:unit && bundle exec parallel_rspec spec/ && manual Playwright visual review in both modes

Decision points:
- Whether to persist theme choice in user profile (DB) or localStorage only
- Whether to support per-component dark mode or only whole-page
- Whether to migrate EasyMDE to a dark-mode-capable editor (or just CSS override)
- Logo strategy: filter:invert, separate dark logo, or SVG currentColor

Anti-patterns:
- Do NOT duplicate the entire Bootstrap 4 stylesheet — only override what changes
- Do NOT use separate dark.css file — use [data-bs-theme] attribute selectors
- Do NOT hardcode dark hex values — use Sass shade-color()/tint-color() in application.scss
- Do NOT break existing light mode appearance

NOT in scope:
- Bootstrap 5 migration (separate epic)
- Vue 3 migration
- Custom color palette redesign
- Per-user theme preference in database (can be added later)

Before closing:
- [ ] Re-read each AC checkbox — verify with evidence
- [ ] Re-read Anti-patterns — confirm none violated
- [ ] Run the exact Verification command — paste output

title: Investigate whether fully anonymized exports of Vulcan projects are desirable
description:
Backup/archive exports embed commenter, triager, and adjudicator attribution (name AND email) per review in backup_serializer.rb:192-211 — independent of membership data. Excluding membership does NOT anonymize comments; imported attribution reappears via commenter_imported_name/email.

Investigate whether an anonymized export mode is desirable (e.g. for sharing archives outside the originating org, or public-comment-review windows where PII scraping is a concern). Decisions to make: export-side redaction flag vs import-side stripping; replacement style (generic role tokens, stable pseudonyms, or fully blank). Note the triage UI already withholds email from row payloads but names are shown and emails persist in the DB.

title: Add duplicate cluster detection — flag near-identical comments for batch triage
description:
Title: Add duplicate cluster detection — flag near-identical comments for batch triage

Description:
68% of Container SRG comments (79 of 116) are near-identical duplicates posted across multiple requirements by the same commenter. The system should auto-detect these clusters and surface them to the triager as "X similar comments from Y — triage as batch?" This uses simple text similarity (first N characters match + same author), not ML. Surfaces as a badge or section in the comments view showing clustered groups the triager can expand and batch-process.

Files:
- Modify: app/models/component.rb (add comment_clusters method)
- Modify: app/blueprints/component_blueprint.rb (expose clusters in show view)
- Create: app/javascript/components/triage/DuplicateClusterPanel.vue
- Modify: app/javascript/components/components/ComponentComments.vue (render cluster panel)
- Test: spec/models/component_spec.rb
- Test: spec/javascript/components/triage/DuplicateClusterPanel.spec.js

First failing test:
it('groups comments with identical first 80 characters from same author into clusters')

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] component.comment_clusters returns groups of 2+ comments with matching text prefix (80 chars) + same user
- [ ] Cluster panel shows at top of comments view: "{N} duplicate clusters detected"
- [ ] Each cluster is expandable showing: author, shared text preview, count, list of rules
- [ ] "Bulk Triage Cluster" button pre-selects all comments in the cluster
- [ ] "Merge Cluster" button opens merge modal pre-populated with cluster members
- [ ] Clusters update when comments are triaged/merged (removed from cluster view)
- [ ] All work via TDD (failing test first)
- [ ] No regressions on existing tests

Verification:
bundle exec rspec spec/models/component_spec.rb && yarn test:unit -- --grep "DuplicateCluster"

Decision points:
- Text similarity threshold: exact first-80-chars match vs fuzzy? Start exact, add fuzzy later.
- Should clusters span across rules or only same-author same-text?

Anti-patterns:
- Do NOT use NLP/ML — simple text prefix matching is sufficient for this pattern
- Do NOT auto-merge clusters — only surface them for admin review
- Do NOT compute clusters on every page load — cache or compute on demand

NOT in scope:
- Cross-commenter similarity detection
- ML-based semantic similarity
- Auto-triage of detected clusters

Before closing:
- [ ] Re-read each AC checkbox — verify with evidence
- [ ] Re-read Anti-patterns — confirm none violated
- [ ] Run the exact Verification command — paste output
- [ ] git diff shows ONLY files listed in Files section

Story points: sp:3
Estimate: 15 min Claude-pace
notes: [2026-05-21] UX Research: Use simple text prefix matching (first 80 chars + same author) — not ML. Surface as inline banner above comments list: 'N duplicate clusters detected'. Each cluster expandable showing author, text preview, count, affected rules. One-click 'Bulk Triage' or 'Merge' from cluster view. Linear-inspired placement.

title: Extract stress-test seeding into dev:stress rake task — replace dummy project
description:
Title: Extract stress-test seeding into dev:stress rake task — replace dummy project

Description:
The "Nothing to See Here" project in db/seeds/data/04_components.rb creates 20 dummy components with random hex names, varied released states, and rule satisfaction links. Its purpose is stress-testing the UI (projects list, component views) but it pollutes demo data with meaningless names. Extract into a parameterized `dev:stress` rake task that creates N projects/components on demand, and remove the dummy block from the seed pipeline.
Design doc: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-05-18-factory-seed-tdd.md (referenced in 04_components.rb TODO comment)

Files:
- Create: lib/tasks/dev_stress.rake
- Modify: db/seeds/data/04_components.rb (remove dummy 20-loop + PoC backfill for dummy components)
- Modify: db/seeds/data/01_projects.rb (remove "Nothing to See Here" project)
- Modify: lib/seed_helpers.rb (update verify! expected counts if needed)
- Modify: docs/development/seed-system.md (add dev:stress documentation)
- Test: spec/tasks/dev_stress_rake_spec.rb (new)

First failing test:
